acceleration_enabled.label: Enable Acceleration Control
acceleration_enabled.tooltip: Enables adjusting the print head acceleration. Increasing the accelerations can reduce printing time at the cost of print quality.

acceleration_flooring.label: Bottom Surface Skin Acceleration
acceleration_flooring.tooltip: The acceleration with which bottom surface skin layers are printed.

acceleration_infill.label: Infill Acceleration
acceleration_infill.tooltip: The acceleration with which infill is printed.

acceleration_ironing.label: Ironing Acceleration
acceleration_ironing.tooltip: The acceleration with which ironing is performed.

acceleration_layer_0.label: Initial Layer Acceleration
acceleration_layer_0.tooltip: The acceleration for the initial layer.

acceleration_prime_tower.label: Prime Tower Acceleration
acceleration_prime_tower.tooltip: The acceleration with which the prime tower is printed.

acceleration_print.label: Print Acceleration
acceleration_print.tooltip: The acceleration with which printing happens.

acceleration_print_layer_0.label: Initial Layer Print Acceleration
acceleration_print_layer_0.tooltip: The acceleration during the printing of the initial layer.

acceleration_roofing.label: Top Surface Skin Acceleration
acceleration_roofing.tooltip: The acceleration with which top surface skin layers are printed.

acceleration_skirt_brim.label: Skirt/Brim Acceleration
acceleration_skirt_brim.tooltip: The acceleration with which the skirt and brim are printed. Normally this is done with the initial layer acceleration, but sometimes you might want to print the skirt or brim at a different acceleration.

acceleration_support.label: Support Acceleration
acceleration_support.tooltip: The acceleration with which the support structure is printed.

acceleration_support_bottom.label: Support Floor Acceleration
acceleration_support_bottom.tooltip: The acceleration with which the floors of support are printed. Printing them at lower acceleration can improve adhesion of support on top of your model.

acceleration_support_infill.label: Support Infill Acceleration
acceleration_support_infill.tooltip: The acceleration with which the infill of support is printed.

acceleration_support_interface.label: Support Interface Acceleration
acceleration_support_interface.tooltip: The acceleration with which the roofs and floors of support are printed. Printing them at lower acceleration can improve overhang quality.

acceleration_support_roof.label: Support Roof Acceleration
acceleration_support_roof.tooltip: The acceleration with which the roofs of support are printed. Printing them at lower acceleration can improve overhang quality.

acceleration_topbottom.label: Top/Bottom Acceleration
acceleration_topbottom.tooltip: The acceleration with which top/bottom layers are printed.

acceleration_travel.label: Travel Acceleration
acceleration_travel.tooltip: The acceleration with which travel moves are made.

acceleration_travel_enabled.label: Enable Travel Acceleration
acceleration_travel_enabled.tooltip: Use a separate acceleration rate for travel moves. If disabled, travel moves will use the acceleration value of the printed line at their destination.

acceleration_travel_layer_0.label: Initial Layer Travel Acceleration
acceleration_travel_layer_0.tooltip: The acceleration for travel moves in the initial layer.

acceleration_wall.label: Wall Acceleration
acceleration_wall.tooltip: The acceleration with which the walls are printed.

acceleration_wall_0.label: Outer Wall Acceleration
acceleration_wall_0.tooltip: The acceleration with which the outermost walls are printed.

acceleration_wall_0_flooring.label: Bottom Surface Outer Wall Acceleration
acceleration_wall_0_flooring.tooltip: The acceleration with which the bottom surface outermost walls are printed.

acceleration_wall_0_roofing.label: Top Surface Outer Wall Acceleration
acceleration_wall_0_roofing.tooltip: The acceleration with which the top surface outermost walls are printed.

acceleration_wall_x.label: Inner Wall Acceleration
acceleration_wall_x.tooltip: The acceleration with which all inner walls are printed.

acceleration_wall_x_flooring.label: Bottom Surface Inner Wall Acceleration
acceleration_wall_x_flooring.tooltip: The acceleration with which the bottom surface inner walls are printed.

acceleration_wall_x_roofing.label: Top Surface Inner Wall Acceleration
acceleration_wall_x_roofing.tooltip: The acceleration with which the top surface inner walls are printed.

adaptive_layer_height_enabled.label: Use Adaptive Layers
adaptive_layer_height_enabled.tooltip: Adaptive layers computes the layer heights depending on the shape of the model.

adaptive_layer_height_threshold.label: Adaptive Layers Topography Size
adaptive_layer_height_threshold.tooltip: Target horizontal distance between two adjacent layers. Reducing this setting causes thinner layers to be used to bring the edges of the layers closer together.

adaptive_layer_height_variation.label: Adaptive Layers Maximum Variation
adaptive_layer_height_variation.tooltip: The maximum allowed height different from the base layer height.

adaptive_layer_height_variation_step.label: Adaptive Layers Variation Step Size
adaptive_layer_height_variation_step.tooltip: The difference in height of the next layer height compared to the previous one.

adhesion_extruder_nr.label: Build Plate Adhesion Extruder
adhesion_extruder_nr.tooltip: The extruder train to use for printing the skirt/brim/raft. This is used in multi-extrusion.

adhesion_type.label: Build Plate Adhesion Type
adhesion_type.tooltip: Different options that help to improve both priming your extrusion and adhesion to the build plate. Brim adds a single layer flat area around the base of your model to prevent warping. Raft adds a thick grid with a roof below the model. Skirt is a line printed around the model, but not connected to the model.

alternate_carve_order.label: Alternate Mesh Removal
alternate_carve_order.tooltip: Switch to which mesh intersecting volumes will belong with every layer, so that the overlapping meshes become interwoven. Turning this setting off will cause one of the meshes to obtain all of the volume in the overlap, while it is removed from the other meshes.

alternate_extra_perimeter.label: Alternate Extra Wall
alternate_extra_perimeter.tooltip: Prints an extra wall at every other layer. This way infill gets caught between these extra walls, resulting in stronger prints.

anti_overhang_mesh.label: Anti Overhang Mesh
anti_overhang_mesh.tooltip: Use this mesh to specify where no part of the model should be detected as overhang. This can be used to remove unwanted support structure.

bottom_layers.label: Bottom Layers
bottom_layers.tooltip: The number of bottom layers. When calculated by the bottom thickness, this value is rounded to a whole number.

bottom_skin_expand_distance.label: Bottom Skin Expand Distance
bottom_skin_expand_distance.tooltip: The distance the bottom skins are expanded into the infill. Higher values makes the skin attach better to the infill pattern and makes the skin adhere better to the walls on the layer below. Lower values save amount of material used.

bottom_skin_preshrink.label: Bottom Skin Removal Width
bottom_skin_preshrink.tooltip: The largest width of bottom skin areas which are to be removed. Every skin area smaller than this value will disappear. This can help in limiting the amount of time and material spent on printing bottom skin at slanted surfaces in the model.

bottom_thickness.label: Bottom Thickness
bottom_thickness.tooltip: The thickness of the bottom layers in the print. This value divided by the layer height defines the number of bottom layers.

bridge_enable_more_layers.label: Bridge Has Multiple Layers
bridge_enable_more_layers.tooltip: If enabled, the second and third layers above the air are printed using the following settings. Otherwise, those layers are printed using the normal settings.

bridge_fan_speed.label: Bridge Fan Speed
bridge_fan_speed.tooltip: Percentage fan speed to use when printing bridge walls and skin.

bridge_fan_speed_2.label: Bridge Second Skin Fan Speed
bridge_fan_speed_2.tooltip: Percentage fan speed to use when printing the second bridge skin layer.

bridge_fan_speed_3.label: Bridge Third Skin Fan Speed
bridge_fan_speed_3.tooltip: Percentage fan speed to use when printing the third bridge skin layer.

bridge_interlace_lines.label: Interlace Bridge Lines
bridge_interlace_lines.tooltip: When enabled, bridging lines will be printed interlaced, i.e. in 2 monotonic passes so that adjacent lines won't be printed just after each other. The lines of the first pass then have more time to cool down and are stronger when the second pass is added.

bridge_settings_enabled.label: Enable Bridge Settings
bridge_settings_enabled.tooltip: Detect bridges and modify print speed, flow and fan settings while bridges are printed.

bridge_skin_density.label: Bridge Skin Density
bridge_skin_density.tooltip: The density of the bridge skin layer. Values less than 100 will increase the gaps between the skin lines.

bridge_skin_density_2.label: Bridge Second Skin Density
bridge_skin_density_2.tooltip: The density of the second bridge skin layer. Values less than 100 will increase the gaps between the skin lines.

bridge_skin_density_3.label: Bridge Third Skin Density
bridge_skin_density_3.tooltip: The density of the third bridge skin layer. Values less than 100 will increase the gaps between the skin lines.

bridge_skin_material_flow.label: Bridge Skin Flow
bridge_skin_material_flow.tooltip: When printing bridge skin regions, the amount of material extruded is multiplied by this value.

bridge_skin_material_flow_2.label: Bridge Second Skin Flow
bridge_skin_material_flow_2.tooltip: When printing the second bridge skin layer, the amount of material extruded is multiplied by this value.

bridge_skin_material_flow_3.label: Bridge Third Skin Flow
bridge_skin_material_flow_3.tooltip: When printing the third bridge skin layer, the amount of material extruded is multiplied by this value.

bridge_skin_speed.label: Bridge Skin Speed
bridge_skin_speed.tooltip: The speed at which bridge skin regions are printed.

bridge_skin_speed_2.label: Bridge Second Skin Speed
bridge_skin_speed_2.tooltip: Print speed to use when printing the second bridge skin layer.

bridge_skin_speed_3.label: Bridge Third Skin Speed
bridge_skin_speed_3.tooltip: Print speed to use when printing the third bridge skin layer.

bridge_skin_support_threshold.label: Bridge Skin Support Threshold
bridge_skin_support_threshold.tooltip: If a skin region is supported for less than this percentage of its area, print it using the bridge settings. Otherwise it is printed using the normal skin settings.

bridge_wall_coast.label: Bridge Wall Coasting
bridge_wall_coast.tooltip: This controls the distance the extruder should coast immediately before a bridge wall begins. Coasting before the bridge starts can reduce the pressure in the nozzle and may produce a flatter bridge.

bridge_wall_material_flow.label: Bridge Wall Flow
bridge_wall_material_flow.tooltip: When printing bridge walls, the amount of material extruded is multiplied by this value.

bridge_wall_min_length.label: Minimum Bridge Wall Length
bridge_wall_min_length.tooltip: Unsupported walls shorter than this will be printed using the normal wall settings. Longer unsupported walls will be printed using the bridge wall settings.

bridge_wall_speed.label: Bridge Wall Speed
bridge_wall_speed.tooltip: The speed at which the bridge walls are printed.

brim_gap.label: Brim Distance
brim_gap.tooltip: The horizontal distance between the first brim line and the outline of the first layer of the print. A small gap can make the brim easier to remove while still providing the thermal benefits.

brim_inside_margin.label: Brim Avoid Margin
brim_inside_margin.tooltip: A brim around a model may touch an other model where you don't want it. This removes all brim within this distance from brimless models.

brim_line_count.label: Brim Line Count
brim_line_count.tooltip: The number of lines used for a brim. More brim lines enhance adhesion to the build plate, but also reduces the effective print area.

brim_location.label: Brim Location
brim_location.tooltip: Print a brim on the outside of the model, inside, or both. Depending on the model, this helps reducing the amount of brim you need to remove afterwards, while ensuring a proper bed adhesion.

brim_replaces_support.label: Brim Replaces Support
brim_replaces_support.tooltip: Enforce brim to be printed around the model even if that space would otherwise be occupied by support. This replaces some regions of the first layer of support by brim regions.

brim_smart_ordering.label: Smart Brim
brim_smart_ordering.tooltip: Swap print order of the innermost and second innermost brim lines. This improves brim removal.

brim_width.label: Brim Width
brim_width.tooltip: The distance from the model to the outermost brim line. A larger brim enhances adhesion to the build plate, but also reduces the effective print area.

build_fan_full_at_height.label: Build Volume Fan Speed at Height
build_fan_full_at_height.tooltip: The height at which the fans spin on regular fan speed. At the layers below the fan speed gradually increases from Initial Fan Speed to Regular Fan Speed.

build_fan_full_layer.label: Build Volume Fan Speed at Layer
build_fan_full_layer.tooltip: The layer at which the build-volume fans spin on full fan speed. This value is calculated and rounded to a whole number.

build_volume_fan_nr.label: Build volume fan number
build_volume_fan_nr.tooltip: The number of the fan that cools the build volume. If this is set to 0, it's means that there is no build volume fan

build_volume_fan_speed.label: Build Volume Fan Speed
build_volume_fan_speed.tooltip: The fan speed (as a percentage) for the auxiliary or build-volume fan, that is set from the moment that the layer specified at 'Build Volume Fan Speed at Layer' is reached and onwards. Before that, the speed is set by 'Initial Layers Build Volume Fan Speed' instead.

build_volume_fan_speed_0.label: Initial Layers Build Volume Fan Speed
build_volume_fan_speed_0.tooltip: The fan speed (as a percentage) for the auxiliary or build-volume fan, that is set until the layer specified at 'Build Volume Fan Speed at Layer' is reached. After that, the speed is set by 'Build Volume Fan Speed' instead (so not this 'Initial Layers' one).

build_volume_temperature.label: Build Volume Temperature
build_volume_temperature.tooltip: The temperature of the environment to print in. If this is 0, the build volume temperature will not be adjusted.

bv_temp_anomaly_limit.label: Build Volume temperature Limit
bv_temp_anomaly_limit.tooltip: Limit on Build Volume temperature Anomaly for detection.

bv_temp_warn_limit.label: Build Volume temperature Warning
bv_temp_warn_limit.tooltip: Limit on Build Volume Temperature warning for detection.

carve_multiple_volumes.label: Remove Mesh Intersection
carve_multiple_volumes.tooltip: Remove areas where multiple meshes are overlapping with each other. This may be used if merged dual material objects overlap with each other.

center_object.label: Center Object
center_object.tooltip: Whether to center the object on the middle of the build platform (0,0), instead of using the coordinate system in which the object was saved.

clean_between_layers.label: Wipe Nozzle Between Layers
clean_between_layers.tooltip: Whether to include nozzle wipe G-Code between layers (maximum 1 per layer). Enabling this setting could influence behavior of retract at layer change. Please use Wipe Retraction settings to control retraction at layers where the wipe script will be working.

coasting_enable.label: Enable Coasting
coasting_enable.tooltip: Coasting replaces the last part of an extrusion path with a travel path. The oozed material is used to print the last piece of the extrusion path in order to reduce stringing.

coasting_min_volume.label: Minimum Volume Before Coasting
coasting_min_volume.tooltip: The smallest volume an extrusion path should have before allowing coasting. For smaller extrusion paths, less pressure has been built up in the bowden tube and so the coasted volume is scaled linearly. This value should always be larger than the Coasting Volume.

coasting_speed.label: Coasting Speed
coasting_speed.tooltip: The speed by which to move during coasting, relative to the speed of the extrusion path. A value slightly under 100% is advised, since during the coasting move the pressure in the bowden tube drops.

coasting_volume.label: Coasting Volume
coasting_volume.tooltip: The volume otherwise oozed. This value should generally be close to the nozzle diameter cubed.

conical_overhang_angle.label: Maximum Model Angle
conical_overhang_angle.tooltip: The maximum angle of overhangs after the they have been made printable. At a value of 0° all overhangs are replaced by a piece of model connected to the build plate, 90° will not change the model in any way.

conical_overhang_enabled.label: Make Overhang Printable
conical_overhang_enabled.tooltip: Change the geometry of the printed model such that minimal support is required. Steep overhangs will become shallow overhangs. Overhanging areas will drop down to become more vertical.

conical_overhang_hole_size.label: Maximum Overhang Hole Area
conical_overhang_hole_size.tooltip: The maximum area of a hole in the base of the model before it's removed by Make Overhang Printable.  Holes smaller than this will be retained.  A value of 0 mm² will fill all holes in the models base.

connect_infill_polygons.label: Connect Infill Polygons
connect_infill_polygons.tooltip: Connect infill paths where they run next to each other. For infill patterns which consist of several closed polygons, enabling this setting greatly reduces the travel time.

connect_skin_polygons.label: Connect Top/Bottom Polygons
connect_skin_polygons.tooltip: Connect top/bottom skin paths where they run next to each other. For the concentric pattern enabling this setting greatly reduces the travel time, but because the connections can happen midway over infill this feature can reduce the top surface quality.

cool_during_extruder_switch.label: Cooling during extruder switch
cool_during_extruder_switch.tooltip:
Whether to activate the cooling fans during a nozzle switch. This can help reducing oozing by cooling the nozzle faster:
  • Unchanged: keep the fans as they were previously
  • Only last extruder: turn on the fan of the last used extruder, but turn the others off (if any). This is useful if you have completely separate extruders.
  • All fans: turn on all fans during nozzle switch. This is useful if you have a single cooling fan, or multiple fans that stay close to each other.

cool_fan_enabled.label: Enable Print Cooling
cool_fan_enabled.tooltip: Enables the print cooling fans while printing. The fans improve print quality on layers with short layer times and bridging / overhangs.

cool_fan_full_at_height.label: Regular Fan Speed at Height
cool_fan_full_at_height.tooltip: The height at which the fans spin on regular fan speed. At the layers below the fan speed gradually increases from Initial Fan Speed to Regular Fan Speed.

cool_fan_full_layer.label: Regular Fan Speed at Layer
cool_fan_full_layer.tooltip: The layer at which the fans spin on regular fan speed. If regular fan speed at height is set, this value is calculated and rounded to a whole number.

cool_fan_speed.label: Fan Speed
cool_fan_speed.tooltip: The speed at which the print cooling fans spin.

cool_fan_speed_0.label: Initial Fan Speed
cool_fan_speed_0.tooltip: The speed at which the fans spin at the start of the print. In subsequent layers the fan speed is gradually increased up to the layer corresponding to Regular Fan Speed at Height.

cool_fan_speed_max.label: Maximum Fan Speed
cool_fan_speed_max.tooltip: The speed at which the fans spin on the minimum layer time. The fan speed gradually increases between the regular fan speed and maximum fan speed when the threshold is hit.

cool_fan_speed_min.label: Regular Fan Speed
cool_fan_speed_min.tooltip: The speed at which the fans spin before hitting the threshold. When a layer prints faster than the threshold, the fan speed gradually inclines towards the maximum fan speed.

cool_lift_head.label: Lift Head
cool_lift_head.tooltip: When the minimum speed is hit because of minimum layer time, lift the head away from the print and wait the extra time until the minimum layer time is reached.

cool_min_layer_time.label: Minimum Layer Time
cool_min_layer_time.tooltip: The minimum time spent in a layer. This forces the printer to slow down, to at least spend the time set here in one layer. This allows the printed material to cool down properly before printing the next layer. Layers may still take shorter than the minimal layer time if Lift Head is disabled and if the Minimum Speed would otherwise be violated.

cool_min_layer_time_fan_speed_max.label: Regular/Maximum Fan Speed Threshold
cool_min_layer_time_fan_speed_max.tooltip: The layer time which sets the threshold between regular fan speed and maximum fan speed. Layers that print slower than this time use regular fan speed. For faster layers the fan speed gradually increases towards the maximum fan speed.

cool_min_layer_time_overhang.label: Minimum Layer Time with Overhang
cool_min_layer_time_overhang.tooltip: The minimum time spent in a layer that contains overhanging extrusions. This forces the printer to slow down, to at least spend the time set here in one layer. This allows the printed material to cool down properly before printing the next layer. Layers may still take shorter than the minimal layer time if Lift Head is disabled and if the Minimum Speed would otherwise be violated.

cool_min_layer_time_overhang_min_segment.label: Minimum Overhang Segment Length
cool_min_layer_time_overhang_min_segment.tooltip: When trying to apply the minimum layer time specific for overhanging layers, it will be applied only if at least one consecutive overhanging extrusion move is longer than this value.

cool_min_speed.label: Minimum Speed
cool_min_speed.tooltip: The minimum print speed, despite slowing down due to the minimum layer time. When the printer would slow down too much, the pressure in the nozzle would be too low and result in bad print quality.

cool_min_temperature.label: Small Layer Printing Temperature
cool_min_temperature.tooltip: Gradually reduce to this temperature when printing at reduced speeds because of minimum layer time.

cross_infill_density_image.label: Cross Infill Density Image
cross_infill_density_image.tooltip: The file location of an image of which the brightness values determine the minimal density at the corresponding location in the infill of the print.

cross_infill_pocket_size.label: Cross 3D Pocket Size
cross_infill_pocket_size.tooltip: The size of pockets at four-way crossings in the cross 3D pattern at heights where the pattern is touching itself.

cross_support_density_image.label: Cross Fill Density Image for Support
cross_support_density_image.tooltip: The file location of an image of which the brightness values determine the minimal density at the corresponding location in the support.

cutting_mesh.label: Cutting Mesh
cutting_mesh.tooltip: Limit the volume of this mesh to within other meshes. You can use this to make certain areas of one mesh print with different settings and with a whole different extruder.

default_material_bed_temperature.label: Default Build Plate Temperature
default_material_bed_temperature.tooltip: The default temperature used for the heated build plate. This should be the "base" temperature of a build plate. All other print temperatures should use offsets based on this value

default_material_print_temperature.label: Default Printing Temperature
default_material_print_temperature.tooltip: The default temperature used for printing. This should be the "base" temperature of a material. All other print temperatures should use offsets based on this value

draft_shield_dist.label: Draft Shield X/Y Distance
draft_shield_dist.tooltip: Distance of the draft shield from the print, in the X/Y directions.

draft_shield_enabled.label: Enable Draft Shield
draft_shield_enabled.tooltip: This will create a wall around the model, which traps (hot) air and shields against exterior airflow. Especially useful for materials which warp easily.

draft_shield_height.label: Draft Shield Height
draft_shield_height.tooltip: Height limitation of the draft shield. Above this height no draft shield will be printed.

draft_shield_height_limitation.label: Draft Shield Limitation
draft_shield_height_limitation.tooltip: Set the height of the draft shield. Choose to print the draft shield at the full height of the model or at a limited height.

expand_skins_expand_distance.label: Skin Expand Distance
expand_skins_expand_distance.tooltip: The distance the skins are expanded into the infill. Higher values makes the skin attach better to the infill pattern and makes the walls on neighboring layers adhere better to the skin. Lower values save amount of material used.

extruder_nr.label: Extruder
extruder_nr.tooltip: The extruder train used for printing. This is used in multi-extrusion.

extruder_prime_pos_abs.label: Absolute Extruder Prime Position
extruder_prime_pos_abs.tooltip: Make the extruder prime position absolute rather than relative to the last-known location of the head.

extruder_prime_pos_x.label: Extruder Prime X Position
extruder_prime_pos_x.tooltip: The X coordinate of the position where the nozzle primes at the start of printing.

extruder_prime_pos_y.label: Extruder Prime Y Position
extruder_prime_pos_y.tooltip: The Y coordinate of the position where the nozzle primes at the start of printing.

extruder_prime_pos_z.label: Extruder Prime Z Position
extruder_prime_pos_z.tooltip: The Z coordinate of the position where the nozzle primes at the start of printing.

extruders_enabled_count.label: Number of Extruders That Are Enabled
extruders_enabled_count.tooltip: Number of extruder trains that are enabled; automatically set in software

fill_outline_gaps.label: Print Thin Walls
fill_outline_gaps.tooltip: Print pieces of the model which are horizontally thinner than the nozzle size.

flooring_angles.label: Bottom Surface Skin Line Directions
flooring_angles.tooltip: A list of integer line directions to use when the bottom surface skin layers use the lines or zig zag pattern. Elements from the list are used sequentially as the layers progress and when the end of the list is reached, it starts at the beginning again. The list items are separated by commas and the whole list is contained in square brackets. Default is an empty list which means use the traditional default angles (45 and 135 degrees).

flooring_extruder_nr.label: Bottom Surface Skin Extruder
flooring_extruder_nr.tooltip: The extruder train used for printing the bottom most skin. This is used in multi-extrusion.

flooring_layer_count.label: Bottom Surface Skin Layers
flooring_layer_count.tooltip: The number of bottom most skin layers. Usually only one bottom most layer is sufficient to generate higher quality bottom surfaces.

flooring_line_width.label: Bottom Surface Skin Line Width
flooring_line_width.tooltip: Width of a single line of the areas at the bottom of the print.

flooring_material_flow.label: Bottom Surface Skin Flow
flooring_material_flow.tooltip: Flow compensation on lines of the areas at the bottom of the print.

flooring_monotonic.label: Monotonic Bottom Surface Order
flooring_monotonic.tooltip: Print bottom surface lines in an ordering that causes them to always overlap with adjacent lines in a single direction. This takes slightly more time to print, but makes flat surfaces look more consistent.

flooring_pattern.label: Bottom Surface Skin Pattern
flooring_pattern.tooltip: The pattern of the bottom most layers.

flow_anomaly_limit.label: Flow Limit
flow_anomaly_limit.tooltip: Limit on flow anomaly for detection.

flow_rate_extrusion_offset_factor.label: Flow Rate Compensation Factor
flow_rate_extrusion_offset_factor.tooltip: How far to move the filament in order to compensate for changes in flow rate, as a percentage of how far the filament would move in one second of extrusion.

flow_rate_max_extrusion_offset.label: Flow Rate Compensation Max Extrusion Offset
flow_rate_max_extrusion_offset.tooltip: The maximum distance in mm to move the filament to compensate for changes in flow rate.

flow_warn_limit.label: Flow Warning
flow_warn_limit.tooltip: Limit on the flow warning for detection.

gantry_height.label: Gantry Height
gantry_height.tooltip: The height difference between the tip of the nozzle and the gantry system (X and Y axes).

gradual_flow_discretisation_step_size.label: Gradual flow discretisation step size
gradual_flow_discretisation_step_size.tooltip: Duration of each step in the gradual flow change

gradual_flow_enabled.label: Gradual flow enabled
gradual_flow_enabled.tooltip: Enable gradual flow changes. When enabled, the flow is gradually increased/decreased to the target flow. This is useful for printers with a bowden tube where the flow is not immediately changed when the extruder motor starts/stops.

gradual_infill_step_height.label: Gradual Infill Step Height
gradual_infill_step_height.tooltip: The height of infill of a given density before switching to half the density.

gradual_infill_steps.label: Gradual Infill Steps
gradual_infill_steps.tooltip: Number of times to reduce the infill density by half when getting further below top surfaces. Areas which are closer to top surfaces get a higher density, up to the Infill Density.

gradual_support_infill_step_height.label: Gradual Support Infill Step Height
gradual_support_infill_step_height.tooltip: The height of support infill of a given density before switching to half the density.

gradual_support_infill_steps.label: Gradual Support Infill Steps
gradual_support_infill_steps.tooltip: Number of times to reduce the support infill density by half when getting further below top surfaces. Areas which are closer to top surfaces get a higher density, up to the Support Infill Density.

group_outer_walls.label: Group Outer Walls
group_outer_walls.tooltip: Outer walls of different islands in the same layer are printed in sequence. When enabled the amount of flow changes is limited because walls are printed one type at a time, when disabled the number of travels between islands is reduced because walls in the same islands are grouped.

hole_xy_offset.label: Hole Horizontal Expansion
hole_xy_offset.tooltip: When greater than zero, the Hole Horizontal Expansion is the amount of offset applied to all holes in each layer. Positive values increase the size of the holes, negative values reduce the size of the holes. When this setting is enabled it can be further tuned with Hole Horizontal Expansion Max Diameter.

hole_xy_offset_max_diameter.label: Hole Horizontal Expansion Max Diameter
hole_xy_offset_max_diameter.tooltip: When greater than zero, the Hole Horizontal Expansion is gradually applied on small holes (small holes are expanded more). When set to zero the Hole Horizontal Expansion will be applied to all holes. Holes larger than the Hole Horizontal Expansion Max Diameter are not expanded.

infill_angles.label: Infill Line Directions
infill_angles.tooltip: A list of integer line directions to use. Elements from the list are used sequentially as the layers progress and when the end of the list is reached, it starts at the beginning again. The list items are separated by commas and the whole list is contained in square brackets. Default is an empty list which means use the traditional default angles (45 and 135 degrees for the lines and zig zag patterns and 45 degrees for all other patterns).

infill_before_walls.label: Infill Before Walls
infill_before_walls.tooltip: Print the infill before printing the walls. Printing the walls first may lead to more accurate walls, but overhangs print worse. Printing the infill first leads to sturdier walls, but the infill pattern might sometimes show through the surface.

infill_enable_travel_optimization.label: Infill Travel Optimization
infill_enable_travel_optimization.tooltip: When enabled, the order in which the infill lines are printed is optimized to reduce the distance travelled. The reduction in travel time achieved very much depends on the model being sliced, infill pattern, density, etc. Note that, for some models that have many small areas of infill, the time to slice the model may be greatly increased.

infill_end_move_inwards_length.label: Infill End Move Inwards Length
infill_end_move_inwards_length.tooltip: When starting or ending infill print, add an inwards extrusion move so that the tips of the infill won't impact the outer wall. This can be useful when the infill is printed at very high speed.

infill_extruder_nr.label: Infill Extruder
infill_extruder_nr.tooltip: The extruder train used for printing infill. This is used in multi-extrusion.

infill_line_distance.label: Infill Line Distance
infill_line_distance.tooltip: Distance between the printed infill lines. This setting is calculated by the infill density and the infill line width.

infill_line_width.label: Infill Line Width
infill_line_width.tooltip: Width of a single infill line.

infill_mesh.label: Infill Mesh
infill_mesh.tooltip: Use this mesh to modify the infill of other meshes with which it overlaps. Replaces infill regions of other meshes with regions for this mesh. It's suggested to only print one Wall and no Top/Bottom Skin for this mesh.

infill_mesh_order.label: Mesh Processing Rank
infill_mesh_order.tooltip: Determines the priority of this mesh when considering multiple overlapping infill meshes. Areas where multiple infill meshes overlap will take on the settings of the mesh with the highest rank. An infill mesh with a higher rank will modify the infill of infill meshes with lower rank and normal meshes.

infill_move_inwards_length.label: Infill Start/End Move Inwards Length
infill_move_inwards_length.tooltip: When starting or ending infill print, add an inwards extrusion move so that the tips of the infill won't impact the outer wall. This can be useful when the infill is printed at very high speed.

infill_multiplier.label: Infill Line Multiplier
infill_multiplier.tooltip: Convert each infill line to this many lines. The extra lines do not cross over each other, but avoid each other. This makes the infill stiffer, but increases print time and material usage.

infill_offset_x.label: Infill X Offset
infill_offset_x.tooltip: The infill pattern is moved this distance along the X axis.

infill_offset_y.label: Infill Y Offset
infill_offset_y.tooltip: The infill pattern is moved this distance along the Y axis.

infill_overlap.label: Infill Overlap Percentage
infill_overlap.tooltip: The amount of overlap between the infill and the walls as a percentage of the infill line width. A slight overlap allows the walls to connect firmly to the infill.

infill_overlap_mm.label: Infill Overlap
infill_overlap_mm.tooltip: The amount of overlap between the infill and the walls. A slight overlap allows the walls to connect firmly to the infill.

infill_pattern.label: Infill Pattern
infill_pattern.tooltip:
The pattern of the infill material of the print.
  • The Line and Zig Zag infill swap direction on alternate layers, reducing material cost.
  • The Grid, Triangle, Tri-Hexagon, Cubic, Octet, Quarter Cubic, Cross, and Concentric patterns are fully printed every layer.
  • Gyroid, Honeycomb, Octagon, Cubic, Quarter Cubic, and Octet infill change with every layer to provide a more equal distribution of strength over each direction.
  • Lightning infill tries to minimize the infill, by only supporting the ceiling of the object.
⚠ Grid, Triangles, and Cubic infill patterns contain intersecting lines, that may cause your nozzle to bump into printed lines, and your printer to vibrate. Use with caution.

infill_sparse_density.label: Infill Density
infill_sparse_density.tooltip: Adjusts the density of infill of the print.

infill_sparse_thickness.label: Infill Layer Thickness
infill_sparse_thickness.tooltip: The thickness per layer of infill material. This value should always be a multiple of the layer height and is otherwise rounded.

infill_start_end_preference.label: Infill Start/End Preference
infill_start_end_preference.tooltip:
• Start at closest: Infill will start at the position that is the closest to the previous position.
  • Start at random: Use a random position for each infill area. This prevents one segment becoming the strongest, but it does so at the cost of an additional travel move.
  • End close to seam: the infill lines will be ordered so that they end as close as possible to the next wall seam. If required, an infill line will be split to provide an appropriate ending position. This helps shortening the travel move between the infill and the wall start position, which should provide a better wall seam, at the cost of adding travel moves during infill print.
    ℹ️ Note that this will be applied only when walls are printed just after infill.

infill_start_move_inwards_length.label: Infill Start Move Inwards Length
infill_start_move_inwards_length.tooltip: When starting infill print, add an inwards extrusion move so that the tips of the infill won't impact the outer wall. This can be useful when the infill is printed at very high speed.

infill_support_angle.label: Infill Overhang Angle
infill_support_angle.tooltip: The minimum angle of internal overhangs for which infill is added. At a value of 0° objects are totally filled with infill, 90° will not provide any infill.

infill_support_enabled.label: Infill Support
infill_support_enabled.tooltip: Print infill structures only where tops of the model should be supported. Enabling this reduces print time and material usage, but leads to ununiform object strength.

infill_wall_line_count.label: Extra Infill Wall Count
infill_wall_line_count.tooltip:
Add extra walls around the infill area. Such walls can make top/bottom skin lines sag down less which means you need less top/bottom skin layers for the same quality at the cost of some extra material.
This feature can combine with the Connect Infill Polygons to connect all the infill into a single extrusion path without the need for travels or retractions if configured right.

infill_wipe_dist.label: Infill Wipe Distance
infill_wipe_dist.tooltip: Distance of a travel move inserted after every infill line, to make the infill stick to the walls better. This option is similar to infill overlap, but without extrusion and only on one end of the infill line.

initial_bottom_layers.label: Initial Bottom Layers
initial_bottom_layers.tooltip: The number of initial bottom layers, from the build-plate upwards. When calculated by the bottom thickness, this value is rounded to a whole number.

initial_layer_inset_direction.label: Initial Layer Wall Ordering
initial_layer_inset_direction.tooltip: Determines the order in which walls are printed on the initial layer. This allows different wall ordering specifically for the first layer to improve bed adhesion. When not changed by user, the Wall Ordering setting value is used.

initial_layer_line_width_factor.label: Initial Layer Line Width
initial_layer_line_width_factor.tooltip: Multiplier of the line width on the first layer. Increasing this could improve bed adhesion.

inset_direction.label: Wall Ordering
inset_direction.tooltip: Determines the order in which walls are printed. Printing outer walls earlier helps with dimensional accuracy, as faults from inner walls cannot propagate to the outside. However printing them later allows them to stack better when overhangs are printed. When there is an uneven amount of total innner walls, the 'center last line' is always printed last.

interlocking_beam_layer_count.label: Interlocking Beam Layer Count
interlocking_beam_layer_count.tooltip: The height of the beams of the interlocking structure, measured in number of layers. Less layers is stronger, but more prone to defects.

interlocking_beam_width.label: Interlocking Beam Width
interlocking_beam_width.tooltip: The width of the interlocking structure beams.

interlocking_boundary_avoidance.label: Interlocking Boundary Avoidance
interlocking_boundary_avoidance.tooltip: The distance from the outside of a model where interlocking structures will not be generated, measured in cells.

interlocking_depth.label: Interlocking Depth
interlocking_depth.tooltip: The distance from the boundary between models to generate interlocking structure, measured in cells. Too few cells will result in poor adhesion.

interlocking_enable.label: Generate Interlocking Structure
interlocking_enable.tooltip: At the locations where models touch, generate an interlocking beam structure. This improves the adhesion between models, especially models printed in different materials.

interlocking_orientation.label: Interlocking Structure Orientation
interlocking_orientation.tooltip: The height of the beams of the interlocking structure, measured in number of layers. Less layers is stronger, but more prone to defects.

ironing_enabled.label: Enable Ironing
ironing_enabled.tooltip: Go over the top surface one additional time, but this time extruding very little material. This is meant to melt the plastic on top further, creating a smoother surface. The pressure in the nozzle chamber is kept high so that the creases in the surface are filled with material.

ironing_flow.label: Ironing Flow
ironing_flow.tooltip: The amount of material, relative to a normal skin line, to extrude during ironing. Keeping the nozzle filled helps filling some of the crevices of the top surface, but too much results in overextrusion and blips on the side of the surface.

ironing_inset.label: Ironing Inset
ironing_inset.tooltip: A distance to keep from the edges of the model. Ironing all the way to the edge of the mesh may result in a jagged edge on your print.

ironing_line_spacing.label: Ironing Line Spacing
ironing_line_spacing.tooltip: The distance between the lines of ironing.

ironing_monotonic.label: Monotonic Ironing Order
ironing_monotonic.tooltip: Print ironing lines in an ordering that causes them to always overlap with adjacent lines in a single direction. This takes slightly more time to print, but makes flat surfaces look more consistent.

ironing_only_highest_layer.label: Iron Only Highest Layer
ironing_only_highest_layer.tooltip: Only perform ironing on the very last layer of the mesh. This saves time if the lower layers don't need a smooth surface finish.

ironing_pattern.label: Ironing Pattern
ironing_pattern.tooltip: The pattern to use for ironing top surfaces.

jerk_enabled.label: Enable Jerk Control
jerk_enabled.tooltip: Enables adjusting the jerk of print head when the velocity in the X or Y axis changes. Increasing the jerk can reduce printing time at the cost of print quality.

jerk_flooring.label: Bottom Surface Skin Jerk
jerk_flooring.tooltip: The maximum instantaneous velocity change with which bottom surface skin layers are printed.

jerk_infill.label: Infill Jerk
jerk_infill.tooltip: The maximum instantaneous velocity change with which infill is printed.

jerk_ironing.label: Ironing Jerk
jerk_ironing.tooltip: The maximum instantaneous velocity change while performing ironing.

jerk_layer_0.label: Initial Layer Jerk
jerk_layer_0.tooltip: The print maximum instantaneous velocity change for the initial layer.

jerk_prime_tower.label: Prime Tower Jerk
jerk_prime_tower.tooltip: The maximum instantaneous velocity change with which the prime tower is printed.

jerk_print.label: Print Jerk
jerk_print.tooltip: The maximum instantaneous velocity change of the print head.

jerk_print_layer_0.label: Initial Layer Print Jerk
jerk_print_layer_0.tooltip: The maximum instantaneous velocity change during the printing of the initial layer.

jerk_roofing.label: Top Surface Skin Jerk
jerk_roofing.tooltip: The maximum instantaneous velocity change with which top surface skin layers are printed.

jerk_skirt_brim.label: Skirt/Brim Jerk
jerk_skirt_brim.tooltip: The maximum instantaneous velocity change with which the skirt and brim are printed.

jerk_support.label: Support Jerk
jerk_support.tooltip: The maximum instantaneous velocity change with which the support structure is printed.

jerk_support_bottom.label: Support Floor Jerk
jerk_support_bottom.tooltip: The maximum instantaneous velocity change with which the floors of support are printed.

jerk_support_infill.label: Support Infill Jerk
jerk_support_infill.tooltip: The maximum instantaneous velocity change with which the infill of support is printed.

jerk_support_interface.label: Support Interface Jerk
jerk_support_interface.tooltip: The maximum instantaneous velocity change with which the roofs and floors of support are printed.

jerk_support_roof.label: Support Roof Jerk
jerk_support_roof.tooltip: The maximum instantaneous velocity change with which the roofs of support are printed.

jerk_topbottom.label: Top/Bottom Jerk
jerk_topbottom.tooltip: The maximum instantaneous velocity change with which top/bottom layers are printed.

jerk_travel.label: Travel Jerk
jerk_travel.tooltip: The maximum instantaneous velocity change with which travel moves are made.

jerk_travel_enabled.label: Enable Travel Jerk
jerk_travel_enabled.tooltip: Use a separate jerk rate for travel moves. If disabled, travel moves will use the jerk value of the printed line at their destination.

jerk_travel_layer_0.label: Initial Layer Travel Jerk
jerk_travel_layer_0.tooltip: The acceleration for travel moves in the initial layer.

jerk_wall.label: Wall Jerk
jerk_wall.tooltip: The maximum instantaneous velocity change with which the walls are printed.

jerk_wall_0.label: Outer Wall Jerk
jerk_wall_0.tooltip: The maximum instantaneous velocity change with which the outermost walls are printed.

jerk_wall_0_flooring.label: Bottom Surface Outer Wall Jerk
jerk_wall_0_flooring.tooltip: The maximum instantaneous velocity change with which the bottom surface outermost walls are printed.

jerk_wall_0_roofing.label: Top Surface Outer Wall Jerk
jerk_wall_0_roofing.tooltip: The maximum instantaneous velocity change with which the top surface outermost walls are printed.

jerk_wall_x.label: Inner Wall Jerk
jerk_wall_x.tooltip: The maximum instantaneous velocity change with which all inner walls are printed.

jerk_wall_x_flooring.label: Bottom Surface Inner Wall Jerk
jerk_wall_x_flooring.tooltip: The maximum instantaneous velocity change with which the bottom surface inner walls are printed.

jerk_wall_x_roofing.label: Top Surface Inner Wall Jerk
jerk_wall_x_roofing.tooltip: The maximum instantaneous velocity change with which the top surface inner walls are printed.

keep_retracting_during_travel.label: Keep Retracting During Travel
keep_retracting_during_travel.tooltip: When retraction during travel is enabled, and there is more than enough time to perform a full retract during a travel move, spread the retraction over the whole travel move with a lower retraction speed, so that we do not travel with a non-retracting nozzle. This can help reducing oozing.

layer_0_max_flow_acceleration.label: Initial layer max flow acceleration
layer_0_max_flow_acceleration.tooltip: Minimum speed for gradual flow changes for the first layer

layer_0_z_overlap.label: Initial Layer Z Overlap
layer_0_z_overlap.tooltip:
Make the first and second layer of the model overlap in the Z direction to compensate for the filament lost in the airgap. All models above the first model layer will be shifted down by this amount.
It may be noted that sometimes the second layer is printed below initial layer because of this setting. This is intended behavior

layer_height.label: Layer Height
layer_height.tooltip: The height of each layer in mm. Higher values produce faster prints in lower resolution, lower values produce slower prints in higher resolution.

layer_height_0.label: Initial Layer Height
layer_height_0.tooltip: The height of the initial layer in mm. A thicker initial layer makes adhesion to the build plate easier.

layer_start_at_z_seam.label: Layer Start at Z Seam
layer_start_at_z_seam.tooltip: Start printing at the Z seam position.

layer_start_x.label: Layer Start X
layer_start_x.tooltip: The X coordinate of the position near where to find the part to start printing each layer.

layer_start_y.label: Layer Start Y
layer_start_y.tooltip: The Y coordinate of the position near where to find the part to start printing each layer.

lightning_infill_overhang_angle.label: Lightning Infill Overhang Angle
lightning_infill_overhang_angle.tooltip: Determines when a lightning infill layer has to support the model above it. Measured in the angle given the thickness.

lightning_infill_prune_angle.label: Lightning Infill Prune Angle
lightning_infill_prune_angle.tooltip: The endpoints of infill lines are shortened to save on material. This setting is the angle of overhang of the endpoints of these lines.

lightning_infill_straightening_angle.label: Lightning Infill Straightening Angle
lightning_infill_straightening_angle.tooltip: The infill lines are straightened out to save on printing time. This is the maximum angle of overhang allowed across the length of the infill line.

lightning_infill_support_angle.label: Lightning Infill Support Angle
lightning_infill_support_angle.tooltip: Determines when a lightning infill layer has to support anything above it. Measured in the angle given the thickness of a layer.

line_width.label: Line Width
line_width.tooltip: Width of a single line. Generally, the width of each line should correspond to the width of the nozzle. However, slightly reducing this value could produce better prints.

machine_acceleration.label: Default Acceleration
machine_acceleration.tooltip: The default acceleration of print head movement.

machine_always_write_active_tool.label: Always Write Active Tool
machine_always_write_active_tool.tooltip: Write active tool after sending temp commands to inactive tool. Required for Dual Extruder printing with Smoothie or other firmware with modal tool commands.

machine_buildplate_type.label: Build Plate Type
machine_buildplate_type.tooltip: The type of build plate installed on the printer.

machine_center_is_zero.label: Is Center Origin
machine_center_is_zero.tooltip: Whether the X/Y coordinates of the zero position of the printer is at the center of the printable area.

machine_depth.label: Machine Depth
machine_depth.tooltip: The depth (Y-direction) of the printable area.

machine_disallowed_areas.label: Disallowed Areas
machine_disallowed_areas.tooltip: A list of polygons with areas the print head is not allowed to enter.

machine_end_gcode.default_value:
M104 S0
M140 S0
;Retract the filament
G92 E1
G1 E-1 F300
G28 X0 Y0
M84
machine_end_gcode.label: End G-code
machine_end_gcode.tooltip: G-code commands to be executed at the very end - separated by \n.

machine_endstop_positive_direction_x.label: X Endstop in Positive Direction
machine_endstop_positive_direction_x.tooltip: Whether the endstop of the X axis is in the positive direction (high X coordinate) or negative (low X coordinate).

machine_endstop_positive_direction_y.label: Y Endstop in Positive Direction
machine_endstop_positive_direction_y.tooltip: Whether the endstop of the Y axis is in the positive direction (high Y coordinate) or negative (low Y coordinate).

machine_endstop_positive_direction_z.label: Z Endstop in Positive Direction
machine_endstop_positive_direction_z.tooltip: Whether the endstop of the Z axis is in the positive direction (high Z coordinate) or negative (low Z coordinate).

machine_extruder_change_duration.label: Extruder Change duration
machine_extruder_change_duration.tooltip: When using a multi tool setup, this value is the tool change time in seconds.  This value will be added to the estimate time based on the number of changes that occur.

machine_extruder_cooling_fan_number.label: Extruder Print Cooling Fan
machine_extruder_cooling_fan_number.tooltip: The number of the print cooling fan associated with this extruder. Only change this from the default value of 0 when you have a different print cooling fan for each extruder.

machine_extruder_count.label: Number of Extruders
machine_extruder_count.tooltip: Number of extruder trains. An extruder train is the combination of a feeder, bowden tube, and nozzle.

machine_extruder_end_code.label: Extruder End G-Code
machine_extruder_end_code.tooltip: End g-code to execute when switching away from this extruder.

machine_extruder_end_code_duration.label: Extruder End G-Code Duration
machine_extruder_end_code_duration.tooltip: The time it takes to execute the end g-code, when switching away from this extruder.

machine_extruder_end_pos_abs.label: Extruder End Position Absolute
machine_extruder_end_pos_abs.tooltip: Make the extruder ending position absolute rather than relative to the last-known location of the head.

machine_extruder_end_pos_x.label: Extruder End Position X
machine_extruder_end_pos_x.tooltip: The x-coordinate of the ending position when turning the extruder off.

machine_extruder_end_pos_y.label: Extruder End Position Y
machine_extruder_end_pos_y.tooltip: The y-coordinate of the ending position when turning the extruder off.

machine_extruder_prestart_code.label: Extruder Prestart G-Code
machine_extruder_prestart_code.tooltip: Prestart g-code to execute before switching to this extruder.

machine_extruder_start_code.label: Extruder Start G-Code
machine_extruder_start_code.tooltip: Start g-code to execute when switching to this extruder.

machine_extruder_start_code_duration.label: Extruder Start G-Code Duration
machine_extruder_start_code_duration.tooltip: The time it'll take to execute the start g-code, when switching to this extruder.

machine_extruder_start_pos_abs.label: Extruder Start Position Absolute
machine_extruder_start_pos_abs.tooltip: Make the extruder starting position absolute rather than relative to the last-known location of the head.

machine_extruder_start_pos_x.label: Extruder Start Position X
machine_extruder_start_pos_x.tooltip: The x-coordinate of the starting position when turning the extruder on.

machine_extruder_start_pos_y.label: Extruder Start Position Y
machine_extruder_start_pos_y.tooltip: The y-coordinate of the starting position when turning the extruder on.

machine_extruders_share_heater.label: Extruders Share Heater
machine_extruders_share_heater.tooltip: Whether the extruders share a single heater rather than each extruder having its own heater.

machine_extruders_share_nozzle.label: Extruders Share Nozzle
machine_extruders_share_nozzle.tooltip: Whether the extruders share a single nozzle rather than each extruder having its own nozzle. When set to true, it is expected that the printer-start gcode script properly sets up all extruders in an initial retraction state that is known and mutually compatible (either zero or one filament not retracted); in that case the initial retraction status is described, per extruder, by the 'machine_extruders_shared_nozzle_initial_retraction' parameter.

machine_extruders_shared_nozzle_initial_.label: Shared Nozzle Initial Retraction
machine_extruders_shared_nozzle_initial_.tooltip: How much the filament of each extruder is assumed to have been retracted from the shared nozzle tip at the completion of the printer-start gcode script; the value should be equal to or greater than the length of the common part of the nozzle's ducts.

machine_feeder_wheel_diameter.label: Feeder Wheel Diameter
machine_feeder_wheel_diameter.tooltip: The diameter of the wheel that drives the material in the feeder.

machine_firmware_retract.label: Firmware Retraction
machine_firmware_retract.tooltip: Whether to use firmware retract commands (G10/G11) instead of using the E property in G1 commands to retract the material.

machine_gcode_flavor.label: G-code Flavor
machine_gcode_flavor.tooltip: The type of g-code to be generated.

machine_head_with_fans_polygon.label: Machine Head & Fan Polygon
machine_head_with_fans_polygon.tooltip: The dimensions of the print head used to determine 'Safe Model Distance' when printing 'One at a Time'. These numbers relate to the centerline of the first extruder nozzle. Left of the nozzle is 'X Min' and must be negative.  Rear of the nozzle is 'Y Min' and must be negative.  X Max (right) and Y Max (front) are positive numbers.  Gantry height is the dimension from the build plate to the X gantry beam.

machine_heat_zone_length.label: Heat Zone Length
machine_heat_zone_length.tooltip: The distance from the tip of the nozzle in which heat from the nozzle is transferred to the filament.

machine_heated_bed.label: Has Heated Build Plate
machine_heated_bed.tooltip: Whether the machine has a heated build plate present.

machine_heated_build_volume.label: Has Build Volume Temperature Stabilization
machine_heated_build_volume.tooltip: Whether the machine is able to stabilize the build volume temperature.

machine_height.label: Machine Height
machine_height.tooltip: The height (Z-direction) of the printable area.

machine_max_acceleration_e.label: Maximum Filament Acceleration
machine_max_acceleration_e.tooltip: Maximum acceleration for the motor of the filament.

machine_max_acceleration_x.label: Maximum Acceleration X
machine_max_acceleration_x.tooltip: Maximum acceleration for the motor of the X-direction

machine_max_acceleration_y.label: Maximum Acceleration Y
machine_max_acceleration_y.tooltip: Maximum acceleration for the motor of the Y-direction.

machine_max_acceleration_z.label: Maximum Acceleration Z
machine_max_acceleration_z.tooltip: Maximum acceleration for the motor of the Z-direction.

machine_max_feedrate_e.label: Maximum Speed E
machine_max_feedrate_e.tooltip: The maximum speed of the filament.

machine_max_feedrate_x.label: Maximum Speed X
machine_max_feedrate_x.tooltip: The maximum speed for the motor of the X-direction.

machine_max_feedrate_y.label: Maximum Speed Y
machine_max_feedrate_y.tooltip: The maximum speed for the motor of the Y-direction.

machine_max_feedrate_z.label: Maximum Speed Z
machine_max_feedrate_z.tooltip: The maximum speed for the motor of the Z-direction.

machine_max_jerk_e.label: Default Filament Jerk
machine_max_jerk_e.tooltip: Default jerk for the motor of the filament.

machine_max_jerk_xy.label: Default X-Y Jerk
machine_max_jerk_xy.tooltip: Default jerk for movement in the horizontal plane.

machine_max_jerk_z.label: Default Z Jerk
machine_max_jerk_z.tooltip: Default jerk for the motor of the Z-direction.

machine_min_cool_heat_time_window.label: Minimal Time Standby Temperature
machine_min_cool_heat_time_window.tooltip: The minimal time an extruder has to be inactive before the nozzle is cooled. Only when an extruder is not used for longer than this time will it be allowed to cool down to the standby temperature.

machine_minimum_feedrate.label: Minimum Feedrate
machine_minimum_feedrate.tooltip: The minimal movement speed of the print head.

machine_name.label: Machine Type
machine_name.tooltip: The name of your 3D printer model.

machine_nozzle_cool_down_speed.label: Cool Down Speed
machine_nozzle_cool_down_speed.tooltip: The speed (°C/s) by which the nozzle cools down averaged over the window of normal printing temperatures and the standby temperature.

machine_nozzle_expansion_angle.label: Nozzle Angle
machine_nozzle_expansion_angle.tooltip: The angle between the horizontal plane and the conical part right above the tip of the nozzle.

machine_nozzle_head_distance.label: Nozzle Length
machine_nozzle_head_distance.tooltip: The height difference between the tip of the nozzle and the lowest part of the print head.

machine_nozzle_heat_up_speed.label: Heat Up Speed
machine_nozzle_heat_up_speed.tooltip: The speed (°C/s) by which the nozzle heats up averaged over the window of normal printing temperatures and the standby temperature.

machine_nozzle_id.label: Nozzle ID
machine_nozzle_id.tooltip: The nozzle ID for an extruder train, such as "AA 0.4" and "BB 0.8".

machine_nozzle_offset_x.label: Nozzle X Offset
machine_nozzle_offset_x.tooltip: The x-coordinate of the offset of the nozzle.

machine_nozzle_offset_y.label: Nozzle Y Offset
machine_nozzle_offset_y.tooltip: The y-coordinate of the offset of the nozzle.

machine_nozzle_size.label: Nozzle Diameter
machine_nozzle_size.tooltip: The inner diameter of the nozzle. Change this setting when using a non-standard nozzle size.

machine_nozzle_temp_enabled.label: Enable Nozzle Temperature Control
machine_nozzle_temp_enabled.tooltip: Whether to control temperature from Cura. Turn this off to control nozzle temperature from outside of Cura.

machine_nozzle_tip_outer_diameter.label: Outer Nozzle Diameter
machine_nozzle_tip_outer_diameter.tooltip: The outer diameter of the tip of the nozzle.

machine_scale_fan_speed_zero_to_one.label: Scale Fan Speed To 0-1
machine_scale_fan_speed_zero_to_one.tooltip: Scale the fan speed to be between 0 and 1 instead of between 0 and 256.

machine_scan_first_layer.label: Scan the first layer
machine_scan_first_layer.tooltip: Whether to scan the first layer for layer adhesion problems.

machine_shape.label: Build Plate Shape
machine_shape.tooltip: The shape of the build plate without taking unprintable areas into account.

machine_show_variants.label: Show Machine Variants
machine_show_variants.tooltip: Whether to show the different variants of this machine, which are described in separate json files.

machine_start_gcode.default_value:
G28 ;Home
G1 Z15.0 F6000 ;Move the platform down 15mm
;Prime the extruder
G92 E0
G1 F200 E3
G92 E0
machine_start_gcode.label: Start G-code
machine_start_gcode.tooltip: G-code commands to be executed at the very start - separated by \n.

machine_start_gcode_first.label: Start GCode must be first
machine_start_gcode_first.tooltip: This setting controls if the start-gcode is forced to always be the first g-code. Without this option other g-code, such as a T0 can be inserted before the start g-code.

machine_steps_per_mm_e.label: Steps per Millimeter (E)
machine_steps_per_mm_e.tooltip: How many steps of the stepper motors will result in moving the feeder wheel by one millimeter around its circumference.

machine_steps_per_mm_x.label: Steps per Millimeter (X)
machine_steps_per_mm_x.tooltip: How many steps of the stepper motor will result in one millimeter of movement in the X direction.

machine_steps_per_mm_y.label: Steps per Millimeter (Y)
machine_steps_per_mm_y.tooltip: How many steps of the stepper motor will result in one millimeter of movement in the Y direction.

machine_steps_per_mm_z.label: Steps per Millimeter (Z)
machine_steps_per_mm_z.tooltip: How many steps of the stepper motor will result in one millimeter of movement in the Z direction.

machine_time_estimation_factor.label: Print Time Estimation Factor
machine_time_estimation_factor.tooltip: Multiplier for adjusting print time estimates. 100% shows original estimate. Higher values indicate longer actual print times.

machine_use_extruder_offset_to_offset_co.label: Offset with Extruder
machine_use_extruder_offset_to_offset_co.tooltip: Apply the extruder offset to the coordinate system. Affects all extruders.

machine_width.label: Machine Width
machine_width.tooltip: The width (X-direction) of the printable area.

magic_fuzzy_skin_enabled.label: Fuzzy Skin
magic_fuzzy_skin_enabled.tooltip: Randomly jitter while printing the outer wall, so that the surface has a rough and fuzzy look.

magic_fuzzy_skin_outside_only.label: Fuzzy Skin Outside Only
magic_fuzzy_skin_outside_only.tooltip: Jitter only the parts' outlines and not the parts' holes.

magic_fuzzy_skin_point_density.label: Fuzzy Skin Density
magic_fuzzy_skin_point_density.tooltip: The average density of points introduced on each polygon in a layer. Note that the original points of the polygon are discarded, so a low density results in a reduction of the resolution.

magic_fuzzy_skin_point_dist.label: Fuzzy Skin Point Distance
magic_fuzzy_skin_point_dist.tooltip: The average distance between the random points introduced on each line segment. Note that the original points of the polygon are discarded, so a high smoothness results in a reduction of the resolution. This value must be higher than half the Fuzzy Skin Thickness.

magic_fuzzy_skin_thickness.label: Fuzzy Skin Thickness
magic_fuzzy_skin_thickness.tooltip: The width within which to jitter. It's advised to keep this below the outer wall width, since the inner walls are unaltered.

magic_mesh_surface_mode.label: Surface Mode
magic_mesh_surface_mode.tooltip: Treat the model as a surface only, a volume, or volumes with loose surfaces. The normal print mode only prints enclosed volumes. "Surface" prints a single wall tracing the mesh surface with no infill and no top/bottom skin. "Both" prints enclosed volumes like normal and any remaining polygons as surfaces.

magic_spiralize.label: Spiralize Outer Contour
magic_spiralize.tooltip: Spiralize smooths out the Z move of the outer edge. This will create a steady Z increase over the whole print. This feature turns a solid model into a single walled print with a solid bottom. This feature should only be enabled when each layer only contains a single part.

material_alternate_walls.label: Alternate Wall Directions
material_alternate_walls.tooltip: Alternate wall directions every other layer and inset. Useful for materials that can build up stress, like for metal printing.

material_anti_ooze_retracted_position.label: Anti-ooze Retracted Position
material_anti_ooze_retracted_position.tooltip: How far the material needs to be retracted before it stops oozing.

material_anti_ooze_retraction_speed.label: Anti-ooze Retraction Speed
material_anti_ooze_retraction_speed.tooltip: How fast the material needs to be retracted during a filament switch to prevent oozing.

material_bed_temp_prepend.label: Include Build Plate Temperature
material_bed_temp_prepend.tooltip: Whether to include build plate temperature commands at the start of the gcode. When the start_gcode already contains build plate temperature commands Cura frontend will automatically disable this setting.

material_bed_temp_wait.label: Wait for Build Plate Heatup
material_bed_temp_wait.tooltip: Whether to insert a command to wait until the build plate temperature is reached at the start.

material_bed_temperature.label: Build Plate Temperature
material_bed_temperature.tooltip: The temperature used for the heated build plate. If this is 0, the build plate is left unheated.

material_bed_temperature_layer_0.label: Build Plate Temperature Initial Layer
material_bed_temperature_layer_0.tooltip: The temperature used for the heated build plate at the first layer. If this is 0, the build plate is left unheated during the first layer.

material_break_preparation_retracted_pos.label: Break Preparation Retracted Position
material_break_preparation_retracted_pos.tooltip: How far the filament can be stretched before it breaks, while heated.

material_break_preparation_speed.label: Break Preparation Retraction Speed
material_break_preparation_speed.tooltip: How fast the filament needs to be retracted just before breaking it off in a retraction.

material_break_preparation_temperature.label: Break Preparation Temperature
material_break_preparation_temperature.tooltip: The temperature used to purge material, should be roughly equal to the highest possible printing temperature.

material_break_retracted_position.label: Break Retracted Position
material_break_retracted_position.tooltip: How far to retract the filament in order to break it cleanly.

material_break_speed.label: Break Retraction Speed
material_break_speed.tooltip: The speed at which to retract the filament in order to break it cleanly.

material_break_temperature.label: Break Temperature
material_break_temperature.tooltip: The temperature at which the filament is broken for a clean break.

material_crystallinity.label: Crystalline Material
material_crystallinity.tooltip: Is this material the type that breaks off cleanly when heated (crystalline), or is it the type that produces long intertwined polymer chains (non-crystalline)?

material_delta_temperature_roofing.label: Temperature Delta Top Surface
material_delta_temperature_roofing.tooltip: A temperature delta to be applied when printing the top-surface layers. This can help with getting a smoother surface when printing at very high speed. Note that it will be applied without waiting for the temperature to be reached, so it should remain low.

material_diameter.label: Diameter
material_diameter.tooltip: Adjusts the diameter of the filament used. Match this value with the diameter of the used filament.

material_end_of_filament_purge_length.label: End of Filament Purge Length
material_end_of_filament_purge_length.tooltip: How much material to use to purge the previous material out of the nozzle (in length of filament) when replacing an empty spool with a fresh spool of the same material.

material_end_of_filament_purge_speed.label: End of Filament Purge Speed
material_end_of_filament_purge_speed.tooltip: How fast to prime the material after replacing an empty spool with a fresh spool of the same material.

material_extrusion_cool_down_speed.label: Extrusion Cool Down Speed Modifier
material_extrusion_cool_down_speed.tooltip: The extra speed by which the nozzle cools while extruding. The same value is used to signify the heat up speed lost when heating up while extruding.

material_final_print_temperature.label: Final Printing Temperature
material_final_print_temperature.tooltip: The temperature to which to already start cooling down just before the end of printing.

material_flow.label: Flow
material_flow.tooltip: Flow compensation: the amount of material extruded is multiplied by this value.

material_flow_layer_0.label: Initial Layer Flow
material_flow_layer_0.tooltip: Flow compensation for the first layer: the amount of material extruded on the initial layer is multiplied by this value.

material_flow_temp_graph.label: Flow Temperature Graph
material_flow_temp_graph.tooltip: Data linking material flow (in mm3 per second) to temperature (degrees Celsius).

material_flush_purge_length.label: Flush Purge Length
material_flush_purge_length.tooltip: How much material to use to purge the previous material out of the nozzle (in length of filament) when switching to a different material.

material_flush_purge_speed.label: Flush Purge Speed
material_flush_purge_speed.tooltip: How fast to prime the material after switching to a different material.

material_guid.label: Material GUID
material_guid.tooltip: GUID of the material. This is set automatically.

material_initial_print_temperature.label: Initial Printing Temperature
material_initial_print_temperature.tooltip: The minimal temperature while heating up to the Printing Temperature at which printing can already start.

material_is_support_material.label: Is support material
material_is_support_material.tooltip: Is this material typically used as a support material during printing.

material_max_flowrate.label: Material Maximum Flow Rate
material_max_flowrate.tooltip: Maximum flow rate that the printer can extrude for the material

material_maximum_park_duration.label: Maximum Park Duration
material_maximum_park_duration.tooltip: How long the material can be kept out of dry storage safely.

material_no_load_move_factor.label: No Load Move Factor
material_no_load_move_factor.tooltip: A factor indicating how much the filament gets compressed between the feeder and the nozzle chamber, used to determine how far to move the material for a filament switch.

material_pressure_advance_factor.label: Pressure advance factor
material_pressure_advance_factor.tooltip: Tuning factor for pressure advance, which is meant to synchronize extrusion with motion

material_print_temp_prepend.label: Include Material Temperatures
material_print_temp_prepend.tooltip: Whether to include nozzle temperature commands at the start of the gcode. When the start_gcode already contains nozzle temperature commands Cura frontend will automatically disable this setting.

material_print_temp_wait.label: Wait for Nozzle Heatup
material_print_temp_wait.tooltip: Whether to wait until the nozzle temperature is reached at the start.

material_print_temperature_layer_0.label: Printing Temperature Initial Layer
material_print_temperature_layer_0.tooltip: The temperature used for printing the first layer.

material_shrinkage_percentage.label: Scaling Factor Shrinkage Compensation
material_shrinkage_percentage.tooltip: To compensate for the shrinkage of the material as it cools down, the model will be scaled with this factor.

material_shrinkage_percentage_xy.label: Horizontal Scaling Factor Shrinkage Compensation
material_shrinkage_percentage_xy.tooltip: To compensate for the shrinkage of the material as it cools down, the model will be scaled with this factor in the XY-direction (horizontally).

material_shrinkage_percentage_z.label: Vertical Scaling Factor Shrinkage Compensation
material_shrinkage_percentage_z.tooltip: To compensate for the shrinkage of the material as it cools down, the model will be scaled with this factor in the Z-direction (vertically).

material_standby_temperature.label: Standby Temperature
material_standby_temperature.tooltip: The temperature of the nozzle when another nozzle is currently used for printing.

max_extrusion_before_wipe.label: Material Volume Between Wipes
max_extrusion_before_wipe.tooltip: Maximum material that can be extruded before another nozzle wipe is initiated. If this value is less than the volume of material required in a layer, the setting has no effect in this layer, i.e. it is limited to one wipe per layer.

max_flow_acceleration.label: Gradual flow max acceleration
max_flow_acceleration.tooltip: Maximum acceleration for gradual flow changes

max_skin_angle_for_expansion.label: Maximum Skin Angle for Expansion
max_skin_angle_for_expansion.tooltip: Top and/or bottom surfaces of your object with an angle larger than this setting, won't have their top/bottom skin expanded. This avoids expanding the narrow skin areas that are created when the model surface has a near vertical slope. An angle of 0° is horizontal and will cause no skin to be expanded, while an angle of 90° is vertical and will cause all skin to be expanded.

mesh_position_x.label: Mesh Position X
mesh_position_x.tooltip: Offset applied to the object in the x direction.

mesh_position_y.label: Mesh Position Y
mesh_position_y.tooltip: Offset applied to the object in the y direction.

mesh_position_z.label: Mesh Position Z
mesh_position_z.tooltip: Offset applied to the object in the z direction. With this you can perform what was used to be called 'Object Sink'.

mesh_rotation_matrix.label: Mesh Rotation Matrix
mesh_rotation_matrix.tooltip: Transformation matrix to be applied to the model when loading it from file.

meshfix_extensive_stitching.label: Extensive Stitching
meshfix_extensive_stitching.tooltip: Extensive stitching tries to stitch up open holes in the mesh by closing the hole with touching polygons. This option can introduce a lot of processing time.

meshfix_fluid_motion_angle.label: Fluid Motion Angle
meshfix_fluid_motion_angle.tooltip: If a toolpath-segment deviates more than this angle from the general motion it is smoothed.

meshfix_fluid_motion_enabled.label: Enable Fluid Motion
meshfix_fluid_motion_enabled.tooltip: When enabled tool paths are corrected for printers with smooth motion planners. Small movements that deviate from the general tool path direction are smoothed to improve fluid motions.

meshfix_fluid_motion_shift_distance.label: Fluid Motion Shift Distance
meshfix_fluid_motion_shift_distance.tooltip: Distance points are shifted to smooth the path

meshfix_fluid_motion_small_distance.label: Fluid Motion Small Distance
meshfix_fluid_motion_small_distance.tooltip: Distance points are shifted to smooth the path

meshfix_keep_open_polygons.label: Keep Disconnected Faces
meshfix_keep_open_polygons.tooltip: Normally Cura tries to stitch up small holes in the mesh and remove parts of a layer with big holes. Enabling this option keeps those parts which cannot be stitched. This option should be used as a last resort option when everything else fails to produce proper g-code.

meshfix_maximum_deviation.label: Maximum Deviation
meshfix_maximum_deviation.tooltip: The maximum deviation allowed when reducing the resolution for the Maximum Resolution setting. If you increase this, the print will be less accurate, but the g-code will be smaller. Maximum Deviation is a limit for Maximum Resolution, so if the two conflict the Maximum Deviation will always be held true.

meshfix_maximum_extrusion_area_deviation.label: Maximum Extrusion Area Deviation
meshfix_maximum_extrusion_area_deviation.tooltip: The maximum extrusion area deviation allowed when removing intermediate points from a straight line. An intermediate point may serve as width-changing point in a long straight line. Therefore, if it is removed, it will cause the line to have a uniform width and, as a result, lose (or gain) a bit of extrusion area. If you increase this you may notice slight under- (or over-) extrusion in between straight parallel walls, as more intermediate width-changing points will be allowed to be removed. Your print will be less accurate, but the g-code will be smaller.

meshfix_maximum_resolution.label: Maximum Resolution
meshfix_maximum_resolution.tooltip: The minimum size of a line segment after slicing. If you increase this, the mesh will have a lower resolution. This may allow the printer to keep up with the speed it has to process g-code and will increase slice speed by removing details of the mesh that it can't process anyway.

meshfix_maximum_travel_resolution.label: Maximum Travel Resolution
meshfix_maximum_travel_resolution.tooltip: The minimum size of a travel line segment after slicing. If you increase this, the travel moves will have less smooth corners. This may allow the printer to keep up with the speed it has to process g-code, but it may cause model avoidance to become less accurate.

meshfix_union_all.label: Union Overlapping Volumes
meshfix_union_all.tooltip: Ignore the internal geometry arising from overlapping volumes within a mesh and print the volumes as one. This may cause unintended internal cavities to disappear.

meshfix_union_all_remove_holes.label: Remove All Holes
meshfix_union_all_remove_holes.tooltip: Remove the holes in each layer and keep only the outside shape. This will ignore any invisible internal geometry. However, it also ignores layer holes which can be viewed from above or below.

min_bead_width.label: Minimum Thin Wall Line Width
min_bead_width.tooltip: Width of the wall that will replace thin features (according to the Minimum Feature Size) of the model. If the Minimum Wall Line Width is thinner than the thickness of the feature, the wall will become as thick as the feature itself.

min_even_wall_line_width.label: Minimum Even Wall Line Width
min_even_wall_line_width.tooltip: The minimum line width for normal polygonal walls. This setting determines at which model thickness we switch from printing a single thin wall line, to printing two wall lines. A higher Minimum Even Wall Line Width leads to a higher maximum odd wall line width. The maximum even wall line width is calculated as Outer Wall Line Width + 0.5 * Minimum Odd Wall Line Width.

min_feature_size.label: Minimum Feature Size
