min_feature_size.tooltip: Minimum thickness of thin features. Model features that are thinner than this value will not be printed, while features thicker than the Minimum Feature Size will be widened to the Minimum Wall Line Width.

min_infill_area.label: Minimum Infill Area
min_infill_area.tooltip: Don't generate areas of infill smaller than this (use skin instead).

min_odd_wall_line_width.label: Minimum Odd Wall Line Width
min_odd_wall_line_width.tooltip: The minimum line width for middle line gap filler polyline walls. This setting determines at which model thickness we switch from printing two wall lines, to printing two outer walls and a single central wall in the middle. A higher Minimum Odd Wall Line Width leads to a higher maximum even wall line width. The maximum odd wall line width is calculated as 2 * Minimum Even Wall Line Width.

min_skin_width_for_expansion.label: Minimum Skin Width for Expansion
min_skin_width_for_expansion.tooltip: Skin areas narrower than this are not expanded. This avoids expanding the narrow skin areas that are created when the model surface has a slope close to the vertical.

min_wall_line_width.label: Minimum Wall Line Width
min_wall_line_width.tooltip: For thin structures around once or twice the nozzle size, the line widths need to be altered to adhere to the thickness of the model. This setting controls the minimum line width allowed for the walls. The minimum line widths inherently also determine the maximum line widths, since we transition from N to N+1 walls at some geometry thickness where the N walls are wide and the N+1 walls are narrow. The widest possible wall line is twice the Minimum Wall Line Width.

minimum_bottom_area.label: Minimum Support Floor Area
minimum_bottom_area.tooltip: Minimum area size for the floors of the support. Polygons which have an area smaller than this value will be printed as normal support.

minimum_interface_area.label: Minimum Support Interface Area
minimum_interface_area.tooltip: Minimum area size for support interface polygons. Polygons which have an area smaller than this value will be printed as normal support.

minimum_polygon_circumference.label: Minimum Polygon Circumference
minimum_polygon_circumference.tooltip: Polygons in sliced layers that have a circumference smaller than this amount will be filtered out. Lower values lead to higher resolution mesh at the cost of slicing time. It is meant mostly for high resolution SLA printers and very tiny 3D models with a lot of details.

minimum_roof_area.label: Minimum Support Roof Area
minimum_roof_area.tooltip: Minimum area size for the roofs of the support. Polygons which have an area smaller than this value will be printed as normal support.

minimum_support_area.label: Minimum Support Area
minimum_support_area.tooltip: Minimum area size for support polygons. Polygons which have an area smaller than this value will not be generated.

mold_angle.label: Mold Angle
mold_angle.tooltip: The angle of overhang of the outer walls created for the mold. 0° will make the outer shell of the mold vertical, while 90° will make the outside of the model follow the contour of the model.

mold_enabled.label: Mold
mold_enabled.tooltip: Print models as a mold, which can be cast in order to get a model which resembles the models on the build plate.

mold_roof_height.label: Mold Roof Height
mold_roof_height.tooltip: The height above horizontal parts in your model which to print mold.

mold_width.label: Minimal Mold Width
mold_width.tooltip: The minimal distance between the outside of the mold and the outside of the model.

multi_material_paint_depth.label: Multi-material Depth
multi_material_paint_depth.tooltip: The depth of the painted details inside the model. A higher depth will provide a better interlocking, but increase slicing time and memory. Set a very high value to go as deep as possible. The actually calculated depth may vary.

multi_material_paint_resolution.label: Multi-material Precision
multi_material_paint_resolution.tooltip: The precision of the details when generating multi-material shapes based on painting data. A lower precision will provide more details, but increase the slicing time and memory.

multiple_mesh_overlap.label: Merged Meshes Overlap
multiple_mesh_overlap.tooltip: Make meshes which are touching each other overlap a bit. This makes them bond together better.

nozzle_disallowed_areas.label: Nozzle Disallowed Areas
nozzle_disallowed_areas.tooltip: A list of polygons with areas the nozzle is not allowed to enter.

ooze_shield_angle.label: Ooze Shield Angle
ooze_shield_angle.tooltip: The maximum angle a part in the ooze shield will have. With 0 degrees being vertical, and 90 degrees being horizontal. A smaller angle leads to less failed ooze shields, but more material.

ooze_shield_dist.label: Ooze Shield Distance
ooze_shield_dist.tooltip: Distance of the ooze shield from the print, in the X/Y directions.

ooze_shield_enabled.label: Enable Ooze Shield
ooze_shield_enabled.tooltip: Enable exterior ooze shield. This will create a shell around the model which is likely to wipe a second nozzle if it's at the same height as the first nozzle.

optimize_wall_printing_order.label: Optimize Wall Printing Order
optimize_wall_printing_order.tooltip: Optimize the order in which walls are printed so as to reduce the number of retractions and the distance travelled. Most parts will benefit from this being enabled but some may actually take longer so please compare the print time estimates with and without optimization. First layer is not optimized when choosing brim as build plate adhesion type.

ppr_enable.label: Enable Print Process Reporting
ppr_enable.tooltip: Enable print process reporting for setting threshold values for possible fault detection.

prime_blob_enable.label: Enable Prime Blob
prime_blob_enable.tooltip: Whether to prime the filament with a blob before printing. Turning this setting on will ensure that the extruder will have material ready at the nozzle before printing. Printing Brim or Skirt can act like priming too, in which case turning this setting off saves some time.

prime_during_travel_ratio.label: Prime During Travel Move
prime_during_travel_ratio.tooltip:
The ratio of priming performed during the travel move, with the remainder completed while the nozzle is stationary, after traveling
  • When 0, the entire priming is performed while stationary, after the travel ends
  • When 100, the entire priming is performed during the travel move, allowing the print to start immediately

prime_tower_base_curve_magnitude.label: Prime Tower Base Slope
prime_tower_base_curve_magnitude.tooltip: The magnitude factor used for the slope of the prime tower base. If you increase this value, the base will become slimmer. If you decrease it, the base will become thicker.

prime_tower_base_height.label: Prime Tower Base Height
prime_tower_base_height.tooltip: The height of the prime tower base. Increasing this value will result in a more sturdy prime tower because the base will be wider. If this setting is too low, the prime tower will not have a sturdy base.

prime_tower_base_size.label: Prime Tower Base Size
prime_tower_base_size.tooltip: The width of the prime tower brim/base. A larger base enhances adhesion to the build plate, but also reduces the effective print area.

prime_tower_brim_enable.label: Prime Tower Base
prime_tower_brim_enable.tooltip: By enabling this setting, your prime-tower will get a brim, even if the model doesn't. If you want a sturdier base for a high tower, you can increase the base height.

prime_tower_enable.label: Enable Prime Tower
prime_tower_enable.tooltip: Print a tower next to the print which serves to prime the material after each nozzle switch.

prime_tower_flow.label: Prime Tower Flow
prime_tower_flow.tooltip: Flow compensation on prime tower lines.

prime_tower_line_width.label: Prime Tower Line Width
prime_tower_line_width.tooltip: Width of a single prime tower line.

prime_tower_max_bridging_distance.label: Prime Tower Maximum Bridging Distance
prime_tower_max_bridging_distance.tooltip: The maximum length of the branches which may be printed over the air.

prime_tower_min_shell_thickness.label: Prime Tower Minimum Shell Thickness
prime_tower_min_shell_thickness.tooltip: The minimum thickness of the prime tower shell. You may increase it to make the prime tower stronger.

prime_tower_min_volume.label: Prime Tower Minimum Volume
prime_tower_min_volume.tooltip: The minimum volume for each layer of the prime tower in order to purge enough material.

prime_tower_mode.label: Prime Tower Type
prime_tower_mode.tooltip:
How to generate the prime tower:
  • Normal: create a bucket in which secondary materials are primed
  • Interleaved: create a prime tower as sparse as possible. This will save time and filament, but is only possible if the used materials adhere to each other

prime_tower_position_x.label: Prime Tower X Position
prime_tower_position_x.tooltip: The x coordinate of the position of the prime tower.

prime_tower_position_y.label: Prime Tower Y Position
prime_tower_position_y.tooltip: The y coordinate of the position of the prime tower.

prime_tower_raft_base_line_spacing.label: Prime Tower Raft Line Spacing
prime_tower_raft_base_line_spacing.tooltip: The distance between the raft lines for the unique prime tower raft layer. Wide spacing makes for easy removal of the raft from the build plate.

prime_tower_size.label: Prime Tower Size
prime_tower_size.tooltip: The width of the prime tower.

prime_tower_wipe_enabled.label: Wipe Inactive Nozzle on Prime Tower
prime_tower_wipe_enabled.tooltip: After printing the prime tower with one nozzle, wipe the oozed material from the other nozzle off on the prime tower.

print_sequence.label: Print Sequence
print_sequence.tooltip: Whether to print all models one layer at a time or to wait for one model to finish, before moving on to the next. One at a time mode is possible if a) only one extruder is enabled and b) all models are separated in such a way that the whole print head can move in between and all models are lower than the distance between the nozzle and the X/Y axes.

print_temp_anomaly_limit.label: Print temperature Limit
print_temp_anomaly_limit.tooltip: Limit on Print Temperature anomaly for detection.

print_temp_warn_limit.label: Print temperature Warning
print_temp_warn_limit.tooltip: Limit on Print temperature warning for detection.

raft_acceleration.label: Raft Print Acceleration
raft_acceleration.tooltip: The acceleration with which the raft is printed.

raft_airgap.label: Raft Air Gap
raft_airgap.tooltip: The gap between the final raft layer and the first layer of the model. Only the first layer is raised by this amount to lower the bonding between the raft layer and the model. Makes it easier to peel off the raft.

raft_base_acceleration.label: Raft Base Print Acceleration
raft_base_acceleration.tooltip: The acceleration with which the base raft layer is printed.

raft_base_extruder_nr.label: Raft Base Extruder
raft_base_extruder_nr.tooltip: The extruder train to use for printing the first layer of the raft. This is used in multi-extrusion.

raft_base_fan_speed.label: Raft Base Fan Speed
raft_base_fan_speed.tooltip: The fan speed for the base raft layer.

raft_base_flow.label: Raft Base Flow
raft_base_flow.tooltip: The amount of material, relative to a normal extrusion line, to extrude during raft base printing. Having an increased flow may improve adhesion and raft structural strength.

raft_base_infill_overlap.label: Raft Base Infill Overlap Percentage
raft_base_infill_overlap.tooltip: The amount of overlap between the infill and the walls of the raft base, as a percentage of the infill line width. A slight overlap allows the walls to connect firmly to the infill.

raft_base_infill_overlap_mm.label: Raft Base Infill Overlap
raft_base_infill_overlap_mm.tooltip: The amount of overlap between the infill and the walls of the raft base. A slight overlap allows the walls to connect firmly to the infill.

raft_base_jerk.label: Raft Base Print Jerk
raft_base_jerk.tooltip: The jerk with which the base raft layer is printed.

raft_base_line_spacing.label: Raft Base Line Spacing
raft_base_line_spacing.tooltip: The distance between the raft lines for the base raft layer. Wide spacing makes for easy removal of the raft from the build plate.

raft_base_line_width.label: Raft Base Line Width
raft_base_line_width.tooltip: Width of the lines in the base raft layer. These should be thick lines to assist in build plate adhesion.

raft_base_margin.label: Raft Base Extra Margin
raft_base_margin.tooltip: If the raft base is enabled, this is the extra raft area around the model which is also given a raft. Increasing this margin will create a stronger raft while using more material and leaving less area for your print.

raft_base_remove_inside_corners.label: Remove Raft Base Inside Corners
raft_base_remove_inside_corners.tooltip: Remove inside corners from the raft base, causing the raft to become convex.

raft_base_smoothing.label: Raft Base Smoothing
raft_base_smoothing.tooltip: This setting controls how much inner corners in the raft base outline are rounded. Inward corners are rounded to a semi circle with a radius equal to the value given here. This setting also removes holes in the raft outline which are smaller than such a circle.

raft_base_speed.label: Raft Base Print Speed
raft_base_speed.tooltip: The speed at which the base raft layer is printed. This should be printed quite slowly, as the volume of material coming out of the nozzle is quite high.

raft_base_thickness.label: Raft Base Thickness
raft_base_thickness.tooltip: Layer thickness of the base raft layer. This should be a thick layer which sticks firmly to the printer build plate.

raft_base_wall_count.label: Raft Base Wall Count
raft_base_wall_count.tooltip: The number of contours to print around the linear pattern in the base layer of the raft.

raft_fan_speed.label: Raft Fan Speed
raft_fan_speed.tooltip: The fan speed for the raft.

raft_flow.label: Raft Flow
raft_flow.tooltip: The amount of material, relative to a normal extrusion line, to extrude during raft printing. Having an increased flow may improve adhesion and raft structural strength.

raft_interface_acceleration.label: Raft Middle Print Acceleration
raft_interface_acceleration.tooltip: The acceleration with which the middle raft layer is printed.

raft_interface_extruder_nr.label: Raft Middle Extruder
raft_interface_extruder_nr.tooltip: The extruder train to use for printing the middle layer of the raft. This is used in multi-extrusion.

raft_interface_fan_speed.label: Raft Middle Fan Speed
raft_interface_fan_speed.tooltip: The fan speed for the middle raft layer.

raft_interface_flow.label: Raft Interface Flow
raft_interface_flow.tooltip: The amount of material, relative to a normal extrusion line, to extrude during raft interface printing. Having an increased flow may improve adhesion and raft structural strength.

raft_interface_infill_overlap.label: Raft Interface Infill Overlap Percentage
raft_interface_infill_overlap.tooltip: The amount of overlap between the infill and the walls of the raft interface, as a percentage of the infill line width. A slight overlap allows the walls to connect firmly to the infill.

raft_interface_infill_overlap_mm.label: Raft Interface Infill Overlap
raft_interface_infill_overlap_mm.tooltip: The amount of overlap between the infill and the walls of the raft interface. A slight overlap allows the walls to connect firmly to the infill.

raft_interface_jerk.label: Raft Middle Print Jerk
raft_interface_jerk.tooltip: The jerk with which the middle raft layer is printed.

raft_interface_layers.label: Raft Middle Layers
raft_interface_layers.tooltip: The number of layers between the base and the surface of the raft. These comprise the main thickness of the raft. Increasing this creates a thicker, sturdier raft.

raft_interface_line_spacing.label: Raft Middle Spacing
raft_interface_line_spacing.tooltip: The distance between the raft lines for the middle raft layer. The spacing of the middle should be quite wide, while being dense enough to support the top raft layers.

raft_interface_line_width.label: Raft Middle Line Width
raft_interface_line_width.tooltip: Width of the lines in the middle raft layer. Making the second layer extrude more causes the lines to stick to the build plate.

raft_interface_margin.label: Raft Middle Extra Margin
raft_interface_margin.tooltip: If the raft middle is enabled, this is the extra raft area around the model which is also given a raft. Increasing this margin will create a stronger raft while using more material and leaving less area for your print.

raft_interface_remove_inside_corners.label: Remove Raft Middle Inside Corners
raft_interface_remove_inside_corners.tooltip: Remove inside corners from the raft middle part, causing the raft to become convex.

raft_interface_smoothing.label: Raft Middle Smoothing
raft_interface_smoothing.tooltip: This setting controls how much inner corners in the raft middle outline are rounded. Inward corners are rounded to a semi circle with a radius equal to the value given here. This setting also removes holes in the raft outline which are smaller than such a circle.

raft_interface_speed.label: Raft Middle Print Speed
raft_interface_speed.tooltip: The speed at which the middle raft layer is printed. This should be printed quite slowly, as the volume of material coming out of the nozzle is quite high.

raft_interface_thickness.label: Raft Middle Thickness
raft_interface_thickness.tooltip: Layer thickness of the middle raft layer.

raft_interface_wall_count.label: Raft Middle Wall Count
raft_interface_wall_count.tooltip: The number of contours to print around the linear pattern in the middle layers of the raft.

raft_interface_z_offset.label: Raft Interface Z Offset
raft_interface_z_offset.tooltip: When printing the first layer of the raft interface, translate by this offset to customize the adhesion between base and interface. A negative offset should improve the adhesion.

raft_jerk.label: Raft Print Jerk
raft_jerk.tooltip: The jerk with which the raft is printed.

raft_margin.label: Raft Extra Margin
raft_margin.tooltip: If the raft is enabled, this is the extra raft area around the model which is also given a raft. Increasing this margin will create a stronger raft while using more material and leaving less area for your print.

raft_remove_inside_corners.label: Remove Raft Inside Corners
raft_remove_inside_corners.tooltip: Remove inside corners from the raft, causing the raft to become convex.

raft_smoothing.label: Raft Smoothing
raft_smoothing.tooltip: This setting controls how much inner corners in the raft outline are rounded. Inward corners are rounded to a semi circle with a radius equal to the value given here. This setting also removes holes in the raft outline which are smaller than such a circle.

raft_speed.label: Raft Print Speed
raft_speed.tooltip: The speed at which the raft is printed.

raft_surface_acceleration.label: Raft Top Print Acceleration
raft_surface_acceleration.tooltip: The acceleration with which the top raft layers are printed.

raft_surface_extruder_nr.label: Raft Top Extruder
raft_surface_extruder_nr.tooltip: The extruder train to use for printing the top layer(s) of the raft. This is used in multi-extrusion.

raft_surface_fan_speed.label: Raft Top Fan Speed
raft_surface_fan_speed.tooltip: The fan speed for the top raft layers.

raft_surface_flow.label: Raft Surface Flow
raft_surface_flow.tooltip: The amount of material, relative to a normal extrusion line, to extrude during raft surface printing. Having an increased flow may improve adhesion and raft structural strength.

raft_surface_infill_overlap.label: Raft Surface Infill Overlap Percentage
raft_surface_infill_overlap.tooltip: The amount of overlap between the infill and the walls of the raft surface, as a percentage of the infill line width. A slight overlap allows the walls to connect firmly to the infill.

raft_surface_infill_overlap_mm.label: Raft Surface Infill Overlap
raft_surface_infill_overlap_mm.tooltip: The amount of overlap between the infill and the walls of the raft surface. A slight overlap allows the walls to connect firmly to the infill.

raft_surface_jerk.label: Raft Top Print Jerk
raft_surface_jerk.tooltip: The jerk with which the top raft layers are printed.

raft_surface_layers.label: Raft Top Layers
raft_surface_layers.tooltip: The number of top layers on top of the 2nd raft layer. These are fully filled layers that the model sits on. 2 layers result in a smoother top surface than 1.

raft_surface_line_spacing.label: Raft Top Spacing
raft_surface_line_spacing.tooltip: The distance between the raft lines for the top raft layers. The spacing should be equal to the line width, so that the surface is solid.

raft_surface_line_width.label: Raft Top Line Width
raft_surface_line_width.tooltip: Width of the lines in the top surface of the raft. These can be thin lines so that the top of the raft becomes smooth.

raft_surface_margin.label: Raft Top Extra Margin
raft_surface_margin.tooltip: If the raft top is enabled, this is the extra raft area around the model which is also given a raft. Increasing this margin will create a stronger raft while using more material and leaving less area for your print.

raft_surface_monotonic.label: Monotonic Raft Top Surface Order
raft_surface_monotonic.tooltip: Print raft top surface lines in an ordering that causes them to always overlap with adjacent lines in a single direction. This takes slightly more time to print, but makes the surface look more consistent, which is also visible on the model bottom surface.

raft_surface_remove_inside_corners.label: Remove Raft Top Inside Corners
raft_surface_remove_inside_corners.tooltip: Remove inside corners from the raft top part, causing the raft to become convex.

raft_surface_smoothing.label: Raft Top Smoothing
raft_surface_smoothing.tooltip: This setting controls how much inner corners in the raft top outline are rounded. Inward corners are rounded to a semi circle with a radius equal to the value given here. This setting also removes holes in the raft outline which are smaller than such a circle.

raft_surface_speed.label: Raft Top Print Speed
raft_surface_speed.tooltip: The speed at which the top raft layers are printed. These should be printed a bit slower, so that the nozzle can slowly smooth out adjacent surface lines.

raft_surface_thickness.label: Raft Top Layer Thickness
raft_surface_thickness.tooltip: Layer thickness of the top raft layers.

raft_surface_wall_count.label: Raft Top Wall Count
raft_surface_wall_count.tooltip: The number of contours to print around the linear pattern in the top layers of the raft.

raft_surface_z_offset.label: Raft Surface Z Offset
raft_surface_z_offset.tooltip: When printing the first layer of the raft surface, translate by this offset to customize the adhesion between interface and surface. A negative offset should improve the adhesion.

raft_wall_count.label: Raft Wall Count
raft_wall_count.tooltip: The number of contours to print around the linear pattern of the raft.

relative_extrusion.label: Relative Extrusion
relative_extrusion.tooltip: Use relative extrusion rather than absolute extrusion. Using relative E-steps makes for easier post-processing of the g-code. However, it's not supported by all printers and it may produce very slight deviations in the amount of deposited material compared to absolute E-steps. Irrespective of this setting, the extrusion mode will always be set to absolute before any g-code script is output.

remove_empty_first_layers.label: Remove Empty First Layers
remove_empty_first_layers.tooltip: Remove empty layers beneath the first printed layer if they are present. Disabling this setting can cause empty first layers if the Slicing Tolerance setting is set to Exclusive or Middle.

reset_flow_duration.label: Reset flow duration
reset_flow_duration.tooltip: For any travel move longer than this value, the material flow is reset to the paths target flow

retract_at_layer_change.label: Retract at Layer Change
retract_at_layer_change.tooltip: Retract the filament when the nozzle is moving to the next layer.

retraction_amount.label: Retraction Distance
retraction_amount.tooltip: The length of material retracted during a retraction move.

retraction_combing.label: Combing Mode
retraction_combing.tooltip: Combing keeps the nozzle within already printed areas when traveling. This results in slightly longer travel moves but reduces the need for retractions. If combing is off, the material will retract and the nozzle moves in a straight line to the next point. It is also possible to avoid combing over top/bottom skin areas or to only comb within the infill.

retraction_combing_avoid_distance.label: Inside Travel Avoid Distance
retraction_combing_avoid_distance.tooltip: The distance between the nozzle and already printed outer walls when travelling inside a model.

retraction_combing_max_distance.label: Max Comb Distance With No Retract
retraction_combing_max_distance.tooltip: When greater than zero, combing travel moves that are longer than this distance will use retraction. If set to zero, there is no maximum and combing moves will not use retraction.

retraction_count_max.label: Maximum Retraction Count
retraction_count_max.tooltip: This setting limits the number of retractions occurring within the minimum extrusion distance window. Further retractions within this window will be ignored. This avoids retracting repeatedly on the same piece of filament, as that can flatten the filament and cause grinding issues.

retraction_during_travel_ratio.label: Retraction During Travel Move
retraction_during_travel_ratio.tooltip:
The ratio of retraction performed during the travel move, with the remainder completed while the nozzle is stationary, before traveling
  • When 0, the entire retraction is performed while stationary, before the travel begins
  • When 100, the entire retraction is performed during the travel move, bypassing the stationary phase

retraction_enable.label: Enable Retraction
retraction_enable.tooltip: Retract the filament when the nozzle is moving over a non-printed area.

retraction_extra_prime_amount.label: Retraction Extra Prime Amount
retraction_extra_prime_amount.tooltip: Some material can ooze away during a travel move, which can be compensated for here.

retraction_extrusion_window.label: Minimum Extrusion Distance Window
retraction_extrusion_window.tooltip: The window in which the maximum retraction count is enforced. This value should be approximately the same as the retraction distance, so that effectively the number of times a retraction passes the same patch of material is limited.

retraction_hop.label: Z Hop Height
retraction_hop.tooltip: The height difference when performing a Z Hop.

retraction_hop_after_extruder_switch.label: Z Hop After Extruder Switch
retraction_hop_after_extruder_switch.tooltip: After the machine switched from one extruder to the other, the build plate is lowered to create clearance between the nozzle and the print. This prevents the nozzle from leaving oozed material on the outside of a print.

retraction_hop_after_extruder_switch_hei.label: Z Hop After Extruder Switch Height
retraction_hop_after_extruder_switch_hei.tooltip: The height difference when performing a Z Hop after extruder switch.

retraction_hop_enabled.label: Z Hop When Retracted
retraction_hop_enabled.tooltip: Whenever a retraction is done, the build plate is lowered to create clearance between the nozzle and the print. It prevents the nozzle from hitting the print during travel moves, reducing the chance to knock the print from the build plate.

retraction_hop_only_when_collides.label: Z Hop Only Over Printed Parts
retraction_hop_only_when_collides.tooltip: Only perform a Z Hop when moving over printed parts which cannot be avoided by horizontal motion by Avoid Printed Parts when Traveling.

retraction_min_travel.label: Retraction Minimum Travel
retraction_min_travel.tooltip: The minimum distance of travel needed for a retraction to happen at all. This helps to get fewer retractions in a small area.

retraction_prime_speed.label: Retraction Prime Speed
retraction_prime_speed.tooltip: The speed at which the filament is primed during a retraction move.

retraction_retract_speed.label: Retraction Retract Speed
retraction_retract_speed.tooltip: The speed at which the filament is retracted during a retraction move.

retraction_speed.label: Retraction Speed
retraction_speed.tooltip: The speed at which the filament is retracted and primed during a retraction move.

roofing_angles.label: Top Surface Skin Line Directions
roofing_angles.tooltip: A list of integer line directions to use when the top surface skin layers use the lines or zig zag pattern. Elements from the list are used sequentially as the layers progress and when the end of the list is reached, it starts at the beginning again. The list items are separated by commas and the whole list is contained in square brackets. Default is an empty list which means use the traditional default angles (45 and 135 degrees).

roofing_expansion.label: Top Surface Expansion
roofing_expansion.tooltip: Determines how much the top surfaces are expanded beneath overlapping surfaces. By adjusting this value, you can ensure that the outer edges of the top surfaces are concealed by the layers above, resulting in a better visual quality, particularly for models with curved surfaces.

roofing_extruder_nr.label: Top Surface Skin Extruder
roofing_extruder_nr.tooltip: The extruder train used for printing the top most skin. This is used in multi-extrusion.

roofing_layer_count.label: Top Surface Skin Layers
roofing_layer_count.tooltip: The number of top most skin layers. Usually only one top most layer is sufficient to generate higher quality top surfaces.

roofing_line_width.label: Top Surface Skin Line Width
roofing_line_width.tooltip: Width of a single line of the areas at the top of the print.

roofing_material_flow.label: Top Surface Skin Flow
roofing_material_flow.tooltip: Flow compensation on lines of the areas at the top of the print.

roofing_monotonic.label: Monotonic Top Surface Order
roofing_monotonic.tooltip: Print top surface lines in an ordering that causes them to always overlap with adjacent lines in a single direction. This takes slightly more time to print, but makes flat surfaces look more consistent.

roofing_pattern.label: Top Surface Skin Pattern
roofing_pattern.tooltip: The pattern of the top most layers.

scarf_joint_seam_length.label: Scarf Seam Length
scarf_joint_seam_length.tooltip: Determines the length of the scarf seam, a seam type that should make the Z seam less visible. Must be higher than 0 to be effective.

scarf_joint_seam_start_height_ratio.label: Scarf Seam Start Height
scarf_joint_seam_start_height_ratio.tooltip: The ratio of the selected layer height at which the scarf seam will begin. A lower number will result in a larger seam height. Must be lower than 100 to be effective.

scarf_split_distance.label: Scarf Seam Step Length
scarf_split_distance.tooltip: Determines the length of each step in the flow change when extruding along the scarf seam. A smaller distance will result in a more precise but also more complex G-code.

seam_overhang_angle.label: Seam Overhanging Wall Angle
seam_overhang_angle.tooltip: Try to prevent seams on walls that overhang more than this angle. When the value is 90, no walls will be treated as overhanging.

skin_angles.label: Top/Bottom Line Directions
skin_angles.tooltip: A list of integer line directions to use when the top/bottom layers use the lines or zig zag pattern. Elements from the list are used sequentially as the layers progress and when the end of the list is reached, it starts at the beginning again. The list items are separated by commas and the whole list is contained in square brackets. Default is an empty list which means use the traditional default angles (45 and 135 degrees).

skin_line_width.label: Top/Bottom Line Width
skin_line_width.tooltip: Width of a single top/bottom line.

skin_material_flow_layer_0.label: Initial Layer Bottom Flow
skin_material_flow_layer_0.tooltip: Flow compensation on bottom lines of the first layer

skin_monotonic.label: Monotonic Top/Bottom Order
skin_monotonic.tooltip: Print top/bottom lines in an ordering that causes them to always overlap with adjacent lines in a single direction. This takes slightly more time to print, but makes flat surfaces look more consistent.

skin_no_small_gaps_heuristic.label: No Skin in Z Gaps
skin_no_small_gaps_heuristic.tooltip: When the model has small vertical gaps of only a few layers, there should normally be skin around those layers in the narrow space. Enable this setting to not generate skin if the vertical gap is very small. This improves printing time and slicing time, but technically leaves infill exposed to the air.

skin_outline_count.label: Extra Skin Wall Count
skin_outline_count.tooltip: Replaces the outermost part of the top/bottom pattern with a number of concentric lines. Using one or two lines improves roofs that start on infill material.

skin_overlap.label: Skin Overlap Percentage
skin_overlap.tooltip: Adjust the amount of overlap between the walls and (the endpoints of) the skin-centerlines, as a percentage of the line widths of the skin lines and the innermost wall. A slight overlap allows the walls to connect firmly to the skin. Note that, given an equal skin and wall line-width, any percentage over 50% may already cause any skin to go past the wall, because at that point the position of the nozzle of the skin-extruder may already reach past the middle of the wall.

skin_overlap_mm.label: Skin Overlap
skin_overlap_mm.tooltip: Adjust the amount of overlap between the walls and (the endpoints of) the skin-centerlines. A slight overlap allows the walls to connect firmly to the skin. Note that, given an equal skin and wall line-width, any value over half the width of the wall may already cause any skin to go past the wall, because at that point the position of the nozzle of the skin-extruder may already reach past the middle of the wall.

skin_preshrink.label: Skin Removal Width
skin_preshrink.tooltip: The largest width of skin areas which are to be removed. Every skin area smaller than this value will disappear. This can help in limiting the amount of time and material spent on printing top/bottom skin at slanted surfaces in the model.

skin_support.label: Infill Skin Support
skin_support.tooltip: Print a solid infill pattern just below skin to avoid printing the skin over air.

skin_support_density.label: Skin Support Density
skin_support_density.tooltip: The density of the skin support layer. Values less than 100 will increase the gaps between the lines.

skin_support_fan_speed.label: Skin Support Fan Speed
skin_support_fan_speed.tooltip: Percentage fan speed to use when printing skin support.

skin_support_interlace_lines.label: Interlace Skin Support Lines
skin_support_interlace_lines.tooltip: When enabled, skin support lines will be printed interlaced, i.e. in 2 monotonic passes so that adjacent lines won't be printed just after each other. The lines of the first pass then have more time to cool down and are stronger when the second pass is added.

skin_support_material_flow.label: Skin Support Flow
skin_support_material_flow.tooltip: When printing skin support regions, the amount of material extruded is multiplied by this value.

skin_support_speed.label: Skin Support Speed
skin_support_speed.tooltip: The speed at which skin support regions are printed.

skirt_brim_extruder_nr.label: Skirt/Brim Extruder
skirt_brim_extruder_nr.tooltip: The extruder train to use for printing the skirt or brim. This is used in multi-extrusion.

skirt_brim_line_width.label: Skirt/Brim Line Width
skirt_brim_line_width.tooltip: Width of a single skirt or brim line.

skirt_brim_material_flow.label: Skirt/Brim Flow
skirt_brim_material_flow.tooltip: Flow compensation on skirt or brim lines.

skirt_brim_minimal_length.label: Skirt/Brim Minimum Length
skirt_brim_minimal_length.tooltip: The minimum length of the skirt or brim. If this length is not reached by all skirt or brim lines together, more skirt or brim lines will be added until the minimum length is reached. Note: If the line count is set to 0 this is ignored.

skirt_brim_speed.label: Skirt/Brim Speed
skirt_brim_speed.tooltip: The speed at which the skirt and brim are printed. Normally this is done at the initial layer speed, but sometimes you might want to print the skirt or brim at a different speed.

skirt_gap.label: Skirt Distance
skirt_gap.tooltip:
The horizontal distance between the skirt and the first layer of the print.
This is the minimum distance. Multiple skirt lines will extend outwards from this distance.

skirt_height.label: Skirt Height
skirt_height.tooltip: Printing the innermost skirt line with multiple layers makes it easy to remove the skirt.

skirt_line_count.label: Skirt Line Count
skirt_line_count.tooltip: Multiple skirt lines help to prime your extrusion better for small models. Setting this to 0 will disable the skirt.

slicing_tolerance.label: Slicing Tolerance
slicing_tolerance.tooltip: Vertical tolerance in the sliced layers. The contours of a layer are normally generated by taking cross sections through the middle of each layer's thickness (Middle). Alternatively each layer can have the areas which fall inside of the volume throughout the entire thickness of the layer (Exclusive) or a layer has the areas which fall inside anywhere within the layer (Inclusive). Inclusive retains the most details, Exclusive makes for the best fit and Middle stays closest to the original surface.

small_feature_max_length.label: Small Feature Max Length
small_feature_max_length.tooltip: Feature outlines that are shorter than this length will be printed using Small Feature Speed.

small_feature_speed_factor.label: Small Feature Speed
small_feature_speed_factor.tooltip: Small features will be printed at this percentage of their normal print speed. Slower printing can help with adhesion and accuracy.

small_feature_speed_factor_0.label: Small Feature Initial Layer Speed
small_feature_speed_factor_0.tooltip: Small features on the first layer will be printed at this percentage of their normal print speed. Slower printing can help with adhesion and accuracy.

small_hole_max_size.label: Small Hole Max Size
small_hole_max_size.tooltip: Holes and part outlines with a diameter smaller than this will be printed using Small Feature Speed.

small_skin_on_surface.label: Small Top/Bottom On Surface
small_skin_on_surface.tooltip: Enable small (up to 'Small Top/Bottom Width') regions on the topmost skinned layer (exposed to air) to be filled with walls instead of the default pattern.

small_skin_width.label: Small Top/Bottom Width
small_skin_width.tooltip: Small top/bottom regions are filled with walls instead of the default top/bottom pattern. This helps to avoids jerky motions. Off for the topmost (air-exposed) layer by default (see 'Small Top/Bottom On Surface').

smooth_spiralized_contours.label: Smooth Spiralized Contours
smooth_spiralized_contours.tooltip: Smooth the spiralized contours to reduce the visibility of the Z seam (the Z seam should be barely visible on the print but will still be visible in the layer view). Note that smoothing will tend to blur fine surface details.

speed_equalize_flow_width_factor.label: Flow Equalization Ratio
speed_equalize_flow_width_factor.tooltip: Extrusion width based correction factor on the speed. At 0% the movement speed is kept constant at the Print Speed. At 100% the movement speed is adjusted so that the flow (in mm³/s) is kept constant, i.e. lines half the normal Line Width are printed twice as fast and lines twice as wide are printed half as fast. A value larger than 100% can help to compensate for the higher pressure required to extrude wide lines.

speed_flooring.label: Bottom Surface Skin Speed
speed_flooring.tooltip: The speed at which bottom surface skin layers are printed.

speed_infill.label: Infill Speed
speed_infill.tooltip: The speed at which infill is printed.

speed_ironing.label: Ironing Speed
speed_ironing.tooltip: The speed at which to pass over the top surface.

speed_layer_0.label: Initial Layer Speed
speed_layer_0.tooltip: The speed for the initial layer. A lower value is advised to improve adhesion to the build plate. Does not affect the build plate adhesion structures themselves, like brim and raft.

speed_prime_tower.label: Prime Tower Speed
speed_prime_tower.tooltip: The speed at which the prime tower is printed. Printing the prime tower slower can make it more stable when the adhesion between the different filaments is suboptimal.

speed_print.label: Print Speed
speed_print.tooltip: The speed at which printing happens.

speed_print_layer_0.label: Initial Layer Print Speed
speed_print_layer_0.tooltip: The speed of printing for the initial layer. A lower value is advised to improve adhesion to the build plate.

speed_roofing.label: Top Surface Skin Speed
speed_roofing.tooltip: The speed at which top surface skin layers are printed.

speed_slowdown_layers.label: Number of Slower Layers
speed_slowdown_layers.tooltip: The first few layers are printed slower than the rest of the model, to get better adhesion to the build plate and improve the overall success rate of prints. The speed is gradually increased over these layers.

speed_support.label: Support Speed
speed_support.tooltip: The speed at which the support structure is printed. Printing support at higher speeds can greatly reduce printing time. The surface quality of the support structure is not important since it is removed after printing.

speed_support_bottom.label: Support Floor Speed
speed_support_bottom.tooltip: The speed at which the floor of support is printed. Printing it at lower speed can improve adhesion of support on top of your model.

speed_support_infill.label: Support Infill Speed
speed_support_infill.tooltip: The speed at which the infill of support is printed. Printing the infill at lower speeds improves stability.

speed_support_interface.label: Support Interface Speed
speed_support_interface.tooltip: The speed at which the roofs and floors of support are printed. Printing them at lower speeds can improve overhang quality.

speed_support_roof.label: Support Roof Speed
speed_support_roof.tooltip: The speed at which the roofs of support are printed. Printing them at lower speeds can improve overhang quality.

speed_topbottom.label: Top/Bottom Speed
speed_topbottom.tooltip: The speed at which top/bottom layers are printed.

speed_travel.label: Travel Speed
speed_travel.tooltip: The speed at which travel moves are made.

speed_travel_layer_0.label: Initial Layer Travel Speed
speed_travel_layer_0.tooltip: The speed of travel moves in the initial layer. A lower value is advised to prevent pulling previously printed parts away from the build plate. The value of this setting can automatically be calculated from the ratio between the Travel Speed and the Print Speed.

speed_wall.label: Wall Speed
speed_wall.tooltip: The speed at which the walls are printed.

speed_wall_0.label: Outer Wall Speed
speed_wall_0.tooltip: The speed at which the outermost walls are printed. Printing the outer wall at a lower speed improves the final skin quality. However, having a large difference between the inner wall speed and the outer wall speed will affect quality in a negative way.

speed_wall_0_flooring.label: Bottom Surface Outer Wall Speed
speed_wall_0_flooring.tooltip: The speed at which the bottom surface outermost wall is printed.

speed_wall_0_roofing.label: Top Surface Outer Wall Speed
speed_wall_0_roofing.tooltip: The speed at which the top surface outermost wall is printed.

speed_wall_x.label: Inner Wall Speed
speed_wall_x.tooltip: The speed at which all inner walls are printed. Printing the inner wall faster than the outer wall will reduce printing time. It works well to set this in between the outer wall speed and the infill speed.

speed_wall_x_flooring.label: Bottom Surface Inner Wall Speed
speed_wall_x_flooring.tooltip: The speed at which the bottom surface inner walls are printed.

speed_wall_x_roofing.label: Top Surface Inner Wall Speed
speed_wall_x_roofing.tooltip: The speed at which the top surface inner walls are printed.

speed_z_hop.label: Z Hop Speed
speed_z_hop.tooltip: The speed at which the vertical Z movement is made for Z Hops. This is typically lower than the print speed since the build plate or machine's gantry is harder to move.

sub_div_rad_add.label: Cubic Subdivision Shell
sub_div_rad_add.tooltip: An addition to the radius from the center of each cube to check for the boundary of the model, as to decide whether this cube should be subdivided. Larger values lead to a thicker shell of small cubes near the boundary of the model.

support_angle.label: Support Overhang Angle
support_angle.tooltip: The minimum angle of overhangs for which support is added. At a value of 0° all overhangs are supported, 90° will not provide any support.

support_bottom_angles.label: Support Floor Line Directions
support_bottom_angles.tooltip: A list of integer line directions to use. Elements from the list are used sequentially as the layers progress and when the end of the list is reached, it starts at the beginning again. The list items are separated by commas and the whole list is contained in square brackets. Default is an empty list which means use the default angles (alternates between 45 and 135 degrees if interfaces are quite thick or 90 degrees).

support_bottom_density.label: Support Floor Density
support_bottom_density.tooltip: The density of the floors of the support structure. A higher value results in better adhesion of the support on top of the model.

support_bottom_distance.label: Support Bottom Distance
support_bottom_distance.tooltip: Distance from the print to the bottom of the support. Note that this is rounded up to the next layer height.

support_bottom_enable.label: Enable Support Floor
support_bottom_enable.tooltip: Generate a dense slab of material between the bottom of the support and the model. This will create a skin between the model and support.

support_bottom_extruder_nr.label: Support Floor Extruder
support_bottom_extruder_nr.tooltip: The extruder train to use for printing the floors of the support. This is used in multi-extrusion.

support_bottom_height.label: Support Floor Thickness
support_bottom_height.tooltip: The thickness of the support floors. This controls the number of dense layers that are printed on top of places of a model on which support rests.

support_bottom_line_distance.label: Support Floor Line Distance
support_bottom_line_distance.tooltip: Distance between the printed support floor lines. This setting is calculated by the Support Floor Density, but can be adjusted separately.

support_bottom_line_width.label: Support Floor Line Width
support_bottom_line_width.tooltip: Width of a single support floor line.

support_bottom_material_flow.label: Support Floor Flow
support_bottom_material_flow.tooltip: Flow compensation on support floor lines.

support_bottom_offset.label: Support Floor Horizontal Expansion
support_bottom_offset.tooltip: Amount of offset applied to the floors of the support.

support_bottom_pattern.label: Support Floor Pattern
support_bottom_pattern.tooltip: The pattern with which the floors of the support are printed.

support_bottom_stair_step_height.label: Support Stair Step Height
support_bottom_stair_step_height.tooltip: The height of the steps of the stair-like bottom of support resting on the model. A low value makes the support harder to remove, but too high values can lead to unstable support structures. Set to zero to turn off the stair-like behaviour.

support_bottom_stair_step_min_slope.label: Support Stair Step Minimum Slope Angle
support_bottom_stair_step_min_slope.tooltip: The minimum slope of the area for stair-stepping to take effect. Low values should make support easier to remove on shallower slopes, but really low values may result in some very counter-intuitive results on other parts of the model.

support_bottom_stair_step_width.label: Support Stair Step Maximum Width
support_bottom_stair_step_width.tooltip: The maximum width of the steps of the stair-like bottom of support resting on the model. A low value makes the support harder to remove, but too high values can lead to unstable support structures.

support_bottom_wall_count.label: Support Bottom Wall Line Count
support_bottom_wall_count.tooltip: The number of walls with which to surround support interface floor. Adding a wall can make support print more reliably and can support overhangs better, but increases print time and material used.

support_brim_enable.label: Enable Support Brim
support_brim_enable.tooltip: Generate a brim within the support infill regions of the first layer. This brim is printed underneath the support, not around it. Enabling this setting increases the adhesion of support to the build plate.

support_brim_line_count.label: Support Brim Line Count
support_brim_line_count.tooltip: The number of lines used for the support brim. More brim lines enhance adhesion to the build plate, at the cost of some extra material.

support_brim_minimum_hole_area.label: Minimum hole area in brim support
support_brim_minimum_hole_area.tooltip: Holes in the support brim that have an area less than this value will be filtered out, resulting in a more consistent brim. Set to 0 to have a fully generated support brim

support_brim_width.label: Support Brim Width
support_brim_width.tooltip: The width of the brim to print underneath the support. A larger brim enhances adhesion to the build plate, at the cost of some extra material.

support_conical_angle.label: Conical Support Angle
support_conical_angle.tooltip: The angle of the tilt of conical support. With 0 degrees being vertical, and 90 degrees being horizontal. Smaller angles cause the support to be more sturdy, but consist of more material. Negative angles cause the base of the support to be wider than the top.

support_conical_enabled.label: Enable Conical Support
support_conical_enabled.tooltip: Make support areas smaller at the bottom than at the overhang.

support_conical_min_width.label: Conical Support Minimum Width
support_conical_min_width.tooltip: Minimum width to which the base of the conical support area is reduced. Small widths can lead to unstable support structures.

support_connect_zigzags.label: Connect Support ZigZags
support_connect_zigzags.tooltip: Connect the ZigZags. This will increase the strength of the zig zag support structure.

support_enable.label: Generate Support
support_enable.tooltip: Generate structures to support parts of the model which have overhangs. Without these structures, such parts would collapse during printing.

support_extruder_nr.label: Support Extruder
support_extruder_nr.tooltip: The extruder train to use for printing the support. This is used in multi-extrusion.

support_extruder_nr_layer_0.label: First Layer Support Extruder
support_extruder_nr_layer_0.tooltip: The extruder train to use for printing the first layer of support infill. This is used in multi-extrusion.

support_fan_enable.label: Fan Speed Override
support_fan_enable.tooltip: When enabled, the print cooling fan speed is altered for the skin regions immediately above the support.

support_infill_angles.label: Support Infill Line Directions
support_infill_angles.tooltip: A list of integer line directions to use. Elements from the list are used sequentially as the layers progress and when the end of the list is reached, it starts at the beginning again. The list items are separated by commas and the whole list is contained in square brackets. Default is an empty list which means use the default angle 0 degrees.

support_infill_density_multiplier_initia.label: Support Infill Density Multiplier Initial Layer
support_infill_density_multiplier_initia.tooltip: Multiplier for the infill on the initial layers of the support. Increasing this may help for bed adhesion.

support_infill_extruder_nr.label: Support Infill Extruder
support_infill_extruder_nr.tooltip: The extruder train to use for printing the infill of the support. This is used in multi-extrusion.

support_infill_multiplier.label: Support Infill Line Multiplier
support_infill_multiplier.tooltip: Convert each support infill line to this many lines. The extra lines do not cross over each other, but avoid each other. This makes the infill stiffer, but increases print time and material usage.

support_infill_rate.label: Support Density
support_infill_rate.tooltip: Adjusts the density of the support structure. A higher value results in better overhangs, but the supports are harder to remove.

support_infill_sparse_thickness.label: Support Infill Layer Thickness
support_infill_sparse_thickness.tooltip: The thickness per layer of support infill material. This value should always be a multiple of the layer height and is otherwise rounded.

support_initial_layer_line_distance.label: Initial Layer Support Line Distance
support_initial_layer_line_distance.tooltip: Distance between the printed initial layer support structure lines. This setting is calculated by the support density.

support_interface_angles.label: Support Interface Line Directions
support_interface_angles.tooltip: A list of integer line directions to use. Elements from the list are used sequentially as the layers progress and when the end of the list is reached, it starts at the beginning again. The list items are separated by commas and the whole list is contained in square brackets. Default is an empty list which means use the default angles (alternates between 45 and 135 degrees if interfaces are quite thick or 90 degrees).

support_interface_density.label: Support Interface Density
support_interface_density.tooltip: Adjusts the density of the roofs and floors of the support structure. A higher value results in better overhangs, but the supports are harder to remove.

support_interface_enable.label: Enable Support Interface
support_interface_enable.tooltip: Generate a dense interface between the model and the support. This will create a skin at the top of the support on which the model is printed and at the bottom of the support, where it rests on the model.

support_interface_extruder_nr.label: Support Interface Extruder
support_interface_extruder_nr.tooltip: The extruder train to use for printing the roofs and floors of the support. This is used in multi-extrusion.

support_interface_height.label: Support Interface Thickness
support_interface_height.tooltip: The thickness of the interface of the support where it touches with the model on the bottom or the top.

support_interface_line_width.label: Support Interface Line Width
support_interface_line_width.tooltip: Width of a single line of support roof or floor.

support_interface_material_flow.label: Support Interface Flow
support_interface_material_flow.tooltip: Flow compensation on lines of support roof or floor.

support_interface_offset.label: Support Interface Horizontal Expansion
support_interface_offset.tooltip: Amount of offset applied to the support interface polygons.

support_interface_pattern.label: Support Interface Pattern
support_interface_pattern.tooltip: The pattern with which the interface of the support with the model is printed.

support_interface_priority.label: Support Interface Priority
support_interface_priority.tooltip: How support interface and support will interact when they overlap. Currently only implemented for support roof.

support_interface_wall_count.label: Support Interface Wall Line Count
support_interface_wall_count.tooltip: The number of walls with which to surround support interface. Adding a wall can make support print more reliably and can support overhangs better, but increases print time and material used.

support_join_distance.label: Support Join Distance
support_join_distance.tooltip: The maximum distance between support structures in the X/Y directions. When separate structures are closer together than this value, the structures merge into one.

support_lightning_angle.label: Support Lightning Angle
support_lightning_angle.tooltip: Determines when a lightning infill layer has to support anything above it. Measured in the angle given the thickness of a layer.

support_lightning_overhang_angle.label: Support Lightning Overhang Angle
support_lightning_overhang_angle.tooltip: Determines when a lightning infill layer has to support the lines above it. Measured in the angle given the thickness.

support_lightning_prune_angle.label: Support Lightning Prune Angle
support_lightning_prune_angle.tooltip: The endpoints of infill lines are shortened to save on material. This setting is the angle of overhang of the endpoints of these lines.

support_lightning_straightening_angle.label: Support Lightning Straightening Angle
support_lightning_straightening_angle.tooltip: The infill lines are straightened out to save on printing time. This is the maximum angle of overhang allowed across the length of the infill line.

support_line_distance.label: Support Line Distance
support_line_distance.tooltip: Distance between the printed support structure lines. This setting is calculated by the support density.

support_line_width.label: Support Line Width
support_line_width.tooltip: Width of a single support structure line.

support_material_flow.label: Support Flow
support_material_flow.tooltip: Flow compensation on support structure lines.

support_mesh.label: Support Mesh
support_mesh.tooltip: Use this mesh to specify support areas. This can be used to generate support structure.

support_mesh_drop_down.label: Drop Down Support Mesh
support_mesh_drop_down.tooltip: Make support everywhere below the support mesh, so that there's no overhang in the support mesh.

support_meshes_present.label: Scene Has Support Meshes
support_meshes_present.tooltip: There are support meshes present in the scene. This setting is controlled by Cura.

support_offset.label: Support Horizontal Expansion
support_offset.tooltip: Amount of offset applied to all support polygons in each layer. Positive values can smooth out the support areas and result in more sturdy support.

support_pattern.label: Support Pattern
support_pattern.tooltip: The pattern of the support structures of the print. The different options available result in sturdy or easy to remove support.

support_roof_angles.label: Support Roof Line Directions
support_roof_angles.tooltip: A list of integer line directions to use. Elements from the list are used sequentially as the layers progress and when the end of the list is reached, it starts at the beginning again. The list items are separated by commas and the whole list is contained in square brackets. Default is an empty list which means use the default angles (alternates between 45 and 135 degrees if interfaces are quite thick or 90 degrees).

support_roof_density.label: Support Roof Density
support_roof_density.tooltip: The density of the roofs of the support structure. A higher value results in better overhangs, but the supports are harder to remove.

support_roof_enable.label: Enable Support Roof
support_roof_enable.tooltip: Generate a dense slab of material between the top of support and the model. This will create a skin between the model and support.

support_roof_extruder_nr.label: Support Roof Extruder
support_roof_extruder_nr.tooltip: The extruder train to use for printing the roofs of the support. This is used in multi-extrusion.

support_roof_height.label: Support Roof Thickness
support_roof_height.tooltip: The thickness of the support roofs. This controls the amount of dense layers at the top of the support on which the model rests.

support_roof_line_distance.label: Support Roof Line Distance
support_roof_line_distance.tooltip: Distance between the printed support roof lines. This setting is calculated by the Support Roof Density, but can be adjusted separately.

support_roof_line_width.label: Support Roof Line Width
support_roof_line_width.tooltip: Width of a single support roof line.

support_roof_material_flow.label: Support Roof Flow
support_roof_material_flow.tooltip: Flow compensation on support roof lines.

support_roof_offset.label: Support Roof Horizontal Expansion
support_roof_offset.tooltip: Amount of offset applied to the roofs of the support.

support_roof_pattern.label: Support Roof Pattern
support_roof_pattern.tooltip: The pattern with which the roofs of the support are printed.

support_roof_wall_count.label: Support Roof Wall Line Count
support_roof_wall_count.tooltip: The number of walls with which to surround support interface roof. Adding a wall can make support print more reliably and can support overhangs better, but increases print time and material used.

support_skip_some_zags.label: Break Up Support In Chunks
support_skip_some_zags.tooltip: Skip some support line connections to make the support structure easier to break away. This setting is applicable to the Zig Zag support infill pattern.

support_skip_zag_per_mm.label: Support Chunk Size
support_skip_zag_per_mm.tooltip: Leave out a connection between support lines once every N millimeter to make the support structure easier to break away.

support_structure.label: Support Structure
support_structure.tooltip: Chooses between the techniques available to generate support. "Normal" support creates a support structure directly below the overhanging parts and drops those areas straight down. "Tree" support creates branches towards the overhanging areas that support the model on the tips of those branches, and allows the branches to crawl around the model to support it from the build plate as much as possible.

support_supported_skin_fan_speed.label: Supported Skin Fan Speed
support_supported_skin_fan_speed.tooltip: Percentage fan speed to use when printing the skin regions immediately above the support. Using a high fan speed can make the support easier to remove.

support_top_distance.label: Support Top Distance
support_top_distance.tooltip: Distance from the top of the support to the print.

support_tower_diameter.label: Tower Diameter
support_tower_diameter.tooltip: The diameter of a special tower.

support_tower_maximum_supported_diameter.label: Maximum Tower-Supported Diameter
support_tower_maximum_supported_diameter.tooltip: Maximum diameter in the X/Y directions of a small area which is to be supported by a specialized support tower.

support_tower_roof_angle.label: Tower Roof Angle
support_tower_roof_angle.tooltip: The angle of a rooftop of a tower. A higher value results in pointed tower roofs, a lower value results in flattened tower roofs.

support_tree_angle.label: Maximum Branch Angle
support_tree_angle.tooltip: The maximum angle of the branches while they grow around the model. Use a lower angle to make them more vertical and more stable. Use a higher angle to be able to have more reach.

support_tree_angle_slow.label: Preferred Branch Angle
support_tree_angle_slow.tooltip: The preferred angle of the branches, when they do not have to avoid the model. Use a lower angle to make them more vertical and more stable. Use a higher angle for branches to merge faster.

support_tree_bp_diameter.label: Initial Layer Diameter
support_tree_bp_diameter.tooltip: Diameter every branch tries to achieve when reaching the buildplate. Improves bed adhesion.

support_tree_branch_diameter.label: Branch Diameter
support_tree_branch_diameter.tooltip: The diameter of the thinnest branches of tree support. Thicker branches are more sturdy. Branches towards the base will be thicker than this.

support_tree_branch_diameter_angle.label: Branch Diameter Angle
support_tree_branch_diameter_angle.tooltip: The angle of the branches' diameter as they gradually become thicker towards the bottom. An angle of 0 will cause the branches to have uniform thickness over their length. A bit of an angle can increase stability of the tree support.

support_tree_branch_reach_limit.label: Optimal Branch Range
support_tree_branch_reach_limit.tooltip: A recomendation to how far branches can move from the points they support. Branches can violate this value to reach their destination (buildplate or a flat part of the model). Lowering this value will make the support more sturdy, but increase the amount of branches (and because of that material usage/print time)

support_tree_limit_branch_reach.label: Limit Branch Reach
support_tree_limit_branch_reach.tooltip: Limit how far each branch should travel from the point it supports. This can make the support more sturdy, but will increase the amount of branches (and because of that material usage/print time)

support_tree_max_diameter.label: Trunk Diameter
support_tree_max_diameter.tooltip: The diameter of the widest branches of tree support. A thicker trunk is more sturdy; a thinner trunk takes up less space on the build plate.

support_tree_max_diameter_increase_by_me.label: Diameter Increase To Model
support_tree_max_diameter_increase_by_me.tooltip: The most the diameter of a branch that has to connect to the model may increase by merging with branches that could reach the buildplate. Increasing this reduces print time, but increases the area of support that rests on model

support_tree_min_height_to_model.label: Minimum Height To Model
support_tree_min_height_to_model.tooltip: How tall a branch has to be if it is placed on the model. Prevents small blobs of support. This setting is ignored when a branch is supporting a support roof.

support_tree_rest_preference.label: Rest Preference
support_tree_rest_preference.tooltip: The preferred placement of the support structures. If structures can't be placed at the preferred location, they will be place elsewhere, even if that means placing them on the model.

support_tree_tip_diameter.label: Tip Diameter
support_tree_tip_diameter.tooltip: The diameter of the top of the tip of the branches of tree support.

support_tree_top_rate.label: Branch Density
support_tree_top_rate.tooltip: Adjusts the density of the support structure used to generate the tips of the branches. A higher value results in better overhangs, but the supports are harder to remove. Use Support Roof for very high values or ensure support density is similarly high at the top.

support_type.label: Support Placement
support_type.tooltip: Adjusts the placement of the support structures. The placement can be set to touching build plate or everywhere. When set to everywhere the support structures will also be printed on the model.

support_use_towers.label: Use Towers
support_use_towers.tooltip: Use specialized towers to support tiny overhang areas. These towers have a larger diameter than the region they support. Near the overhang the towers' diameter decreases, forming a roof.

support_wall_count.label: Support Wall Line Count
support_wall_count.tooltip: The number of walls with which to surround support infill. Adding a wall can make support print more reliably and can support overhangs better, but increases print time and material used.

support_xy_distance.label: Support X/Y Distance
support_xy_distance.tooltip: Distance of the support structure from the print in the X/Y directions.

support_xy_distance_overhang.label: Minimum Support X/Y Distance
support_xy_distance_overhang.tooltip: Distance of the support structure from the overhang in the X/Y directions.

support_xy_overrides_z.label: Support Distance Priority
support_xy_overrides_z.tooltip: Whether the Support X/Y Distance overrides the Support Z Distance or vice versa. When X/Y overrides Z the X/Y distance can push away the support from the model, influencing the actual Z distance to the overhang. We can disable this by not applying the X/Y distance around overhangs.

support_z_distance.label: Support Z Distance
support_z_distance.tooltip: Distance from the top/bottom of the support structure to the print. This gap provides clearance to remove the supports after the model is printed. The topmost support layer below the model might be a fraction of regular layers.

support_z_seam_away_from_model.label: Support Z Seam Away from Model
support_z_seam_away_from_model.tooltip: Manage the spatial relationship between the z seam of the support structure and the actual 3D model. This control is crucial as it allows users to ensure the seamless removal of support structures post-printing, without inflicting damage or leaving marks on the printed model.

support_z_seam_min_distance.label: Min Z Seam Distance from Model
support_z_seam_min_distance.tooltip: The distance between the model and its support structure at the z-axis seam.

support_zag_skip_count.label: Support Chunk Line Count
support_zag_skip_count.tooltip: Skip one in every N connection lines to make the support structure easier to break away.

switch_extruder_extra_prime_amount.label: Nozzle Switch Extra Prime Amount
switch_extruder_extra_prime_amount.tooltip: Extra material to prime after nozzle switching.

switch_extruder_prime_speed.label: Nozzle Switch Prime Speed
switch_extruder_prime_speed.tooltip: The speed at which the filament is pushed back after a nozzle switch retraction.

switch_extruder_retraction_amount.label: Nozzle Switch Retraction Distance
switch_extruder_retraction_amount.tooltip: The amount of retraction when switching extruders. Set to 0 for no retraction at all. This should generally be the same as the length of the heat zone.

switch_extruder_retraction_speed.label: Nozzle Switch Retract Speed
switch_extruder_retraction_speed.tooltip: The speed at which the filament is retracted during a nozzle switch retract.

switch_extruder_retraction_speeds.label: Nozzle Switch Retraction Speed
switch_extruder_retraction_speeds.tooltip: The speed at which the filament is retracted. A higher retraction speed works better, but a very high retraction speed can lead to filament grinding.

top_bottom_extruder_nr.label: Top/Bottom Extruder
top_bottom_extruder_nr.tooltip: The extruder train used for printing the top and bottom skin. This is used in multi-extrusion.

top_bottom_pattern.label: Top/Bottom Pattern
top_bottom_pattern.tooltip: The pattern of the top/bottom layers.

top_bottom_pattern_0.label: Bottom Pattern Initial Layer
top_bottom_pattern_0.tooltip: The pattern on the bottom of the print on the first layer.

top_bottom_thickness.label: Top/Bottom Thickness
top_bottom_thickness.tooltip: The thickness of the top/bottom layers in the print. This value divided by the layer height defines the number of top/bottom layers.

top_layers.label: Top Layers
top_layers.tooltip: The number of top layers. When calculated by the top thickness, this value is rounded to a whole number.

top_skin_expand_distance.label: Top Skin Expand Distance
top_skin_expand_distance.tooltip: The distance the top skins are expanded into the infill. Higher values makes the skin attach better to the infill pattern and makes the walls on the layer above adhere better to the skin. Lower values save amount of material used.

top_skin_preshrink.label: Top Skin Removal Width
top_skin_preshrink.tooltip: The largest width of top skin areas which are to be removed. Every skin area smaller than this value will disappear. This can help in limiting the amount of time and material spent on printing top skin at slanted surfaces in the model.

top_thickness.label: Top Thickness
top_thickness.tooltip: The thickness of the top layers in the print. This value divided by the layer height defines the number of top layers.

travel_avoid_distance.label: Travel Avoid Distance
travel_avoid_distance.tooltip: The distance between the nozzle and already printed parts when avoiding during travel moves.

travel_avoid_other_parts.label: Avoid Printed Parts When Traveling
travel_avoid_other_parts.tooltip: The nozzle avoids already printed parts when traveling. This option is only available when combing is enabled.

travel_avoid_supports.label: Avoid Supports When Traveling
travel_avoid_supports.tooltip: The nozzle avoids already printed supports when traveling. This option is only available when combing is enabled.

travel_retract_before_outer_wall.label: Retract Before Outer Wall
travel_retract_before_outer_wall.tooltip:
Retraction behavior when moving to the start of an outer wall:
  • Automatic: Retraction will be calculated according to other settings
  • Always: All the travel moves to outer walls will be retracted
  • Never: All the travel moves to outer walls will not be retracted
  • From infill unretracted: The travel move from an infill area to an outer wall will be unretracted, others will use automatic

user_defined_print_order_enabled.label: Set Print Sequence Manually
user_defined_print_order_enabled.tooltip: Allows you to order the object list to manually set the print sequence. First object from the list will be printed first.

wall_0_acceleration.label: Outer Wall Start Acceleration
wall_0_acceleration.tooltip: This is the acceleration with which to reach the top speed when printing an outer wall.

wall_0_deceleration.label: Outer Wall End Deceleration
wall_0_deceleration.tooltip: This is the deceleration with which to end printing an outer wall.

wall_0_end_speed_ratio.label: Outer Wall End Speed Ratio
wall_0_end_speed_ratio.tooltip: This is the ratio of the top speed to end with when printing an outer wall.

wall_0_extruder_nr.label: Outer Wall Extruder
wall_0_extruder_nr.tooltip: The extruder train used for printing the outer wall. This is used in multi-extrusion.

wall_0_inset.label: Outer Wall Inset
wall_0_inset.tooltip: Inset applied to the path of the outer wall. If the outer wall is smaller than the nozzle, and printed after the inner walls, use this offset to get the hole in the nozzle to overlap with the inner walls instead of the outside of the model.

wall_0_material_flow.label: Outer Wall Flow
wall_0_material_flow.tooltip: Flow compensation on the outermost wall line.

wall_0_material_flow_flooring.label: Bottom Surface Outer Wall Flow
wall_0_material_flow_flooring.tooltip: Flow compensation on the bottom surface outermost wall line.

wall_0_material_flow_layer_0.label: Initial Layer Outer Wall Flow
wall_0_material_flow_layer_0.tooltip: Flow compensation on the outermost wall line of the first layer.

wall_0_material_flow_roofing.label: Top Surface Outer Wall Flow
wall_0_material_flow_roofing.tooltip: Flow compensation on the top surface outermost wall line.

wall_0_speed_split_distance.label: Outer Wall Speed Split Distance
wall_0_speed_split_distance.tooltip: This is the maximum length of an extrusion path when splitting a longer path to apply the outer wall acceleration/deceleration. A smaller distance will create a more precise but also more verbose G-Code.

wall_0_start_speed_ratio.label: Outer Wall Start Speed Ratio
wall_0_start_speed_ratio.tooltip: This is the ratio of the top speed to start with when printing an outer wall.

wall_0_wipe_dist.label: Outer Wall Wipe Distance
wall_0_wipe_dist.tooltip: Distance of a travel move inserted after the outer wall, to hide the Z seam better.

wall_distribution_count.label: Wall Distribution Count
wall_distribution_count.tooltip: The number of walls, counted from the center, over which the variation needs to be spread. Lower values mean that the outer walls don't change in width.

wall_extruder_nr.label: Wall Extruder
wall_extruder_nr.tooltip: The extruder train used for printing the walls. This is used in multi-extrusion.

wall_line_count.label: Wall Line Count
wall_line_count.tooltip: The number of walls. When calculated by the wall thickness, this value is rounded to a whole number.

wall_line_width.label: Wall Line Width
wall_line_width.tooltip: Width of a single wall line.

wall_line_width_0.label: Outer Wall Line Width
wall_line_width_0.tooltip: Width of the outermost wall line. By lowering this value, higher levels of detail can be printed.

wall_line_width_x.label: Inner Wall(s) Line Width
wall_line_width_x.tooltip: Width of a single wall line for all wall lines except the outermost one.

wall_overhang_angle.label: Overhanging Wall Angle
wall_overhang_angle.tooltip: Walls that overhang more than this angle will be printed using overhanging wall settings. When the value is 90, no walls will be treated as overhanging. Overhang that gets supported by support will not be treated as overhang either. Furthermore, any line that's less than half overhanging will also not be treated as overhang.

wall_overhang_speed_factors.label: Overhanging Wall Speeds
wall_overhang_speed_factors.tooltip: Overhanging walls will be printed at a percentage of their normal print speed. You can specify multiple values, so that even more overhanging walls will be printed even slower, e.g. by setting [75, 50, 25]

wall_thickness.label: Wall Thickness
wall_thickness.tooltip: The thickness of the walls in the horizontal direction. This value divided by the wall line width defines the number of walls.

wall_transition_angle.label: Wall Transitioning Threshold Angle
wall_transition_angle.tooltip: When to create transitions between even and odd numbers of walls. A wedge shape with an angle greater than this setting will not have transitions and no walls will be printed in the center to fill the remaining space. Reducing this setting reduces the number and length of these center walls, but may leave gaps or overextrude.

wall_transition_filter_deviation.label: Wall Transitioning Filter Margin
wall_transition_filter_deviation.tooltip: Prevent transitioning back and forth between one extra wall and one less. This margin extends the range of line widths which follow to [Minimum Wall Line Width - Margin, 2 * Minimum Wall Line Width + Margin]. Increasing this margin reduces the number of transitions, which reduces the number of extrusion starts/stops and travel time. However, large line width variation can lead to under- or overextrusion problems.

wall_transition_filter_distance.label: Wall Transitioning Filter Distance
wall_transition_filter_distance.tooltip: If it would be transitioning back and forth between different numbers of walls in quick succession, don't transition at all. Remove transitions if they are closer together than this distance.

wall_transition_length.label: Wall Transition Length
wall_transition_length.tooltip: When transitioning between different numbers of walls as the part becomes thinner, a certain amount of space is allotted to split or join the wall lines.

wall_x_extruder_nr.label: Inner Wall Extruder
wall_x_extruder_nr.tooltip: The extruder train used for printing the inner walls. This is used in multi-extrusion.

wall_x_material_flow.label: Inner Wall(s) Flow
wall_x_material_flow.tooltip: Flow compensation on wall lines for all wall lines except the outermost one.

wall_x_material_flow_flooring.label: Bottom Surface Inner Wall(s) Flow
wall_x_material_flow_flooring.tooltip: Flow compensation on bottom surface wall lines for all wall lines except the outermost one.

wall_x_material_flow_layer_0.label: Initial Layer Inner Wall Flow
wall_x_material_flow_layer_0.tooltip: Flow compensation on wall lines for all wall lines except the outermost one, but only for the first layer

wall_x_material_flow_roofing.label: Top Surface Inner Wall(s) Flow
wall_x_material_flow_roofing.tooltip: Flow compensation on top surface wall lines for all wall lines except the outermost one.

wipe_brush_pos_x.label: Wipe Brush X Position
wipe_brush_pos_x.tooltip: X location where wipe script will start.

wipe_hop_amount.label: Wipe Z Hop Height
wipe_hop_amount.tooltip: The height difference when performing a Z Hop.
