wipe_hop_enable.label: Wipe Z Hop
wipe_hop_enable.tooltip: When wiping, the build plate is lowered to create clearance between the nozzle and the print. It prevents the nozzle from hitting the print during travel moves, reducing the chance to knock the print from the build plate.

wipe_hop_speed.label: Wipe Hop Speed
wipe_hop_speed.tooltip: Speed to move the z-axis during the hop.

wipe_move_distance.label: Wipe Move Distance
wipe_move_distance.tooltip: The distance to move the head back and forth across the brush.

wipe_repeat_count.label: Wipe Repeat Count
wipe_repeat_count.tooltip: Number of times to move the nozzle across the brush.

wipe_retraction_amount.label: Wipe Retraction Distance
wipe_retraction_amount.tooltip: Amount to retract the filament so it does not ooze during the wipe sequence.

wipe_retraction_enable.label: Wipe Retraction Enable
wipe_retraction_enable.tooltip: Retract the filament when the nozzle is moving over a non-printed area.

wipe_retraction_extra_prime_amount.label: Wipe Retraction Extra Prime Amount
wipe_retraction_extra_prime_amount.tooltip: Some material can ooze away during a wipe travel moves, which can be compensated for here.

wipe_retraction_prime_speed.label: Wipe Retraction Prime Speed
wipe_retraction_prime_speed.tooltip: The speed at which the filament is primed during a wipe retraction move.

wipe_retraction_retract_speed.label: Wipe Retraction Retract Speed
wipe_retraction_retract_speed.tooltip: The speed at which the filament is retracted during a wipe retraction move.

wipe_retraction_speed.label: Wipe Retraction Speed
wipe_retraction_speed.tooltip: The speed at which the filament is retracted and primed during a wipe retraction move.

xy_offset.label: Horizontal Expansion
xy_offset.tooltip: Amount of offset applied to all polygons in each layer. Positive values can compensate for too big holes; negative values can compensate for too small holes.

xy_offset_layer_0.label: Initial Layer Horizontal Expansion
xy_offset_layer_0.tooltip: Amount of offset applied to all polygons in the first layer. A negative value can compensate for squishing of the first layer known as "elephant's foot".

z_seam_corner.label: Seam Corner Preference
z_seam_corner.tooltip: Control how corners on the model outline influence the position of the seam. Hide Seam makes the seam more likely to occur on an inside corner. Expose Seam makes the seam more likely to occur on an outside corner. Hide or Expose Seam makes the seam more likely to occur at an inside or outside corner. Smart Hiding allows both inside and outside corners, but chooses inside corners more frequently, if appropriate.

z_seam_on_vertex.label: Z Seam On Vertex
z_seam_on_vertex.tooltip: Place the z-seam on a polygon vertex. Switching this off can place the seam between vertices as well. (Keep in mind that this won't override the restrictions on placing the seam on an unsupported overhang.)

z_seam_position.label: Z Seam Position
z_seam_position.tooltip: The position near where to start printing each part in a layer. Note: For the 'Middle' option, the reference point differs critically based on Z Seam Relative setting - it will be the middle of the buildplate (absolute) or the middle of each object (relative), resulting in completely different seam placements.

z_seam_relative.label: Z Seam Relative
z_seam_relative.tooltip: When enabled, the z seam coordinates are relative to each part's centre. When disabled, the coordinates define an absolute position on the build plate. This setting is critical for the 'Middle' Z Seam Position option: relative positioning places the seam at the middle of each object, while absolute positioning places it at the middle of the buildplate, resulting in completely different seam placements.

z_seam_type.label: Z Seam Alignment
z_seam_type.tooltip: Starting point of each path in a layer. When paths in consecutive layers start at the same point a vertical seam may show on the print. When aligning these near a user specified location, the seam is easiest to remove. When placed randomly the inaccuracies at the paths' start will be less noticeable. When taking the shortest path the print will be quicker.

z_seam_x.label: Z Seam X
z_seam_x.tooltip: The X coordinate of the position near where to start printing each part in a layer. When Z Seam Relative is disabled, this is an absolute position on the buildplate. When enabled, this is relative to each object's center.

z_seam_y.label: Z Seam Y
z_seam_y.tooltip: The Y coordinate of the position near where to start printing each part in a layer. When Z Seam Relative is disabled, this is an absolute position on the buildplate. When enabled, this is relative to each object's center.

zig_zaggify_infill.label: Connect Infill Lines
zig_zaggify_infill.tooltip: Connect the ends where the infill pattern meets the inner wall using a line which follows the shape of the inner wall. Enabling this setting can make the infill adhere to the walls better and reduce the effects of infill on the quality of vertical surfaces. Disabling this setting reduces the amount of material used.

zig_zaggify_support.label: Connect Support Lines
zig_zaggify_support.tooltip: Connect the ends of the support lines together. Enabling this setting can make your support more sturdy and reduce underextrusion, but it will cost more material.
